1	"""Kimi §2/§5 — the POST-FETCH, receipt-emitting ELIGIBILITY JUDGE keyed to the
2	clause ledger. This is the "smart" enforcement layer both prior reviewers missed.
3	
4	The problem it solves (Kimi §5 "single most important change"): the deterministic
5	metadata predicates (dates, ontology-mapped kinds, named-source hosts, tier/peer-
6	review quality) enforce only the CLOSED world. A hard clause the ontology cannot
7	map — "only news and company press releases", "high-quality journal articles",
8	"industry white papers" — lands verbatim in ``RetrievalPolicy.opaque_eligibility``
9	as *disclosed-but-unenforced* (Stage-1 parking lot). This module makes those
10	clauses BITE without an ontology entry: it READS each fetched source and decides,
11	per hard clause, ``pass``/``fail``/``unknown`` with a receipt.
12	
13	Architecture (Kimi §2 "the symmetric design"):
14	
15	  * DETERMINISTIC owns the high-precision predicates it CAN decide — dates (via
16	    metadata), languages, named-source host identity — evaluated FIRST, no LLM.
17	    A clause the ontology already maps is handled by the scope/quality masks
18	    upstream; only the residue reaches the LLM leg.
19	
20	  * A SCHEMA-CONSTRAINED LLM JUDGE owns the open world: for each source, ONE
21	    bounded call scores ALL its still-pending hard clauses at once, reading the
22	    source metadata + body surface (title, venue, host, type, statement, quote)
23	    against each clause verbatim. Reasoning-first safe (big ``max_tokens``,
24	    reasoning captured). Per-clause ``{verdict, basis}`` JSON.
25	
26	Every decision emits a :class:`~src.polaris_graph.retrieval.quality_eligibility.SourceReceipt`
27	(reused verbatim — same ``contract_hash``/``term_id``/``source_id``/``stage``/
28	``verdict``/``basis`` shape) so opaque receipts flow into the SAME
29	``eligibility_receipts.json`` sink and the SAME ``contract_compliance.audit_contract``
30	that quality/topicality already use.
31	
32	AGGREGATION -> citable eligibility (Kimi §2, spec item 2): a source is CITABLE
33	only if it PASSES every HARD "only/exclude/require" clause it is subject to. A
34	``fail`` on any hard clause removes it from the citable menu (KEPT in corpus +
35	diagnostics — §-1.3 disclose-don't-delete). ``unknown`` under a hard OPAQUE clause
36	is **fail-OPEN** (an opaque deontic clause the judge cannot decide must NOT
37	silently quarantine — MEMORY's no-silent-fallback + the topicality fail-open
38	contract), UNLESS the caller marks it fail-closed. SOFT/prefer clauses demote a
39	rank weight, never exclude.
40	
41	CRITICAL — entirely UPSTREAM of the frozen verifier. It only decides which rows
42	are ELIGIBLE to enter the citable menu (``evidence_for_gen``) BEFORE
43	``strict_verify`` enumerates the pool. It NEVER changes HOW a claim is verified.
44	``provenance_generator.py`` / ``strict_verify`` stay 0-diff.
45	
46	The whole module no-ops when there are no opaque hard clauses (byte-identical),
47	and the LLM leg is only reached when a real ``llm_judge`` callable is injected —
48	the eval harness injects a deterministic fake, so the offline eval never fetches.
49	"""
50	
51	from __future__ import annotations
52	
53	import json
54	import re
55	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
56	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
57	from urllib.parse import urlparse
58	
59	# Reuse the EXACT receipt + verdict vocabulary quality/topicality already emit, so
60	# the opaque receipts are indistinguishable at the audit seam except by ``stage``.
61	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.quality_eligibility import (
62	    FAIL,
63	    PASS,
64	    SourceReceipt,
65	    UNKNOWN,
66	    _row_get,
67	    _row_text,
68	    _row_url,
69	)
70	
71	# The stage tag routes opaque receipts separately from quality/topicality at the
72	# compliance audit (Kimi: "keep the opaque stage distinct so audit can route it").
73	STAGE_OPAQUE = "opaque_eligibility"
74	
75	# A per-clause verdict from the LLM judge is one of these (schema-constrained).
76	_LLM_VERDICTS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({PASS, FAIL, UNKNOWN})
77	
78	
79	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
80	# Clause identity — a stable id per opaque clause (so receipts + audit key on it)
81	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
82	
83	
84	def _clause_id(clause: str) -> str:
85	    """A stable, human-legible term id for one verbatim opaque clause.
86	
87	    ``"only news and company press releases"`` -> ``"opaque:only-news-and-compan"``.
88	    Deterministic + collision-resistant enough for a per-run receipt key (the full
89	    clause text travels in the receipt basis, so the id need only disambiguate).
90	    """
91	    slug = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", (clause or "").strip().lower()).strip("-")
92	    return "opaque:" + (slug[:24] or "clause")
93	
94	
95	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
96	# DETERMINISTIC predicates (Kimi §2: "high-precision predicates where they exist")
97	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
98	#
99	# These fire ONLY when a clause is unambiguously a date/language/named-host
100	# constraint that carries its own decidable metadata. Anything else is left for
101	# the LLM leg (returns None here -> "not deterministically decidable").
102	
103	
104	_NAMED_HOST_RE = re.compile(
105	    r"\b((?:[a-z0-9-]+\.)+[a-z]{2,})\b", re.IGNORECASE
106	)
107	
108	
109	def _host_of(url: str) -> str:
110	    try:
111	        return (urlparse(url or "").netloc or "").lower().lstrip("www.")
112	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
113	        return ""
114	
115	
116	def _deterministic_verdict(clause: str, row: Any) -> Optional[tuple[str, str]]:
117	    """Try to decide ``clause`` for ``row`` with NO LLM. Returns ``(verdict, basis)``
118	    or ``None`` when the clause is not a deterministically-decidable predicate.
119	
120	    Only two high-precision classes are handled here (dates + ontology-mapped kinds
121	    are already enforced by the scope/date masks UPSTREAM of this judge — the
122	    opaque residue by construction excludes them). This leg handles:
123	
124	      * a bare **named-host** clause ("use reuters.com", "only from apnews.com") —
125	        host identity match against the row url. This is high precision: a literal
126	        domain token in the clause that either matches or does not match the row's
127	        host.
128	    """
129	    low = (clause or "").strip().lower()
130	    if not low:
131	        return None
132	
133	    # Named-host: the clause names a concrete domain (contains a dotted host token).
134	    hosts_in_clause = [
135	        h.lower().lstrip("www.")
136	        for h in _NAMED_HOST_RE.findall(low)
137	        # ignore accidental "e.g." / "u.s." style tokens (no TLD-ish tail)
138	        if "." in h and not h.endswith(".")
139	    ]
140	    if hosts_in_clause:
141	        row_host = _host_of(_row_url(row))
142	        if not row_host:
143	            return UNKNOWN, "named-host clause but row has no resolvable host"
144	        # An "only/use X" clause => the row host must be (a suffix of) one named host.
145	        matched = any(
146	            row_host == h or row_host.endswith("." + h) or h.endswith("." + row_host)
147	            for h in hosts_in_clause
148	        )
149	        if matched:
150	            return PASS, f"row host {row_host!r} matches named host in clause"
151	        return FAIL, (
152	            f"row host {row_host!r} matches none of the named hosts "
153	            f"{hosts_in_clause!r} in the clause"
154	        )
155	
156	    return None  # not a deterministically-decidable predicate -> LLM leg
157	
158	
159	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
160	# The LLM judge prompt (schema-constrained; one call per source, all clauses)
161	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
162	
163	
164	def _source_metadata_view(row: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
165	    """The domain-neutral metadata surface the judge READS. Every field is already
166	    fetched by live retrieval; the body surface mirrors ``quality_eligibility._row_text``
167	    (statement + direct_quote) — the fetched-body proxy at this seam.
168	    """
169	    url = _row_url(row)
170	    return {
171	        "url": url,
172	        "host": _host_of(url),
173	        "title": str(_row_get(row, "source_title", "") or _row_get(row, "title", "") or ""),
174	        "venue": str(_row_get(row, "openalex_venue", "") or ""),
175	        "source_type": str(_row_get(row, "openalex_source_type", "") or ""),
176	        "is_peer_reviewed": _row_get(row, "is_peer_reviewed", None),
177	        "tier": str(_row_get(row, "tier", "") or ""),
178	        "year": _row_get(row, "year", None) or _row_get(row, "publication_year", None),
179	        "doi": str(_row_get(row, "doi", "") or ""),
180	        # the fetched-body proxy (bounded) — statement + direct_quote.
181	        "body_snippet": _row_text(row)[:1200],
182	    }
183	
184	
185	def _build_judge_prompt(meta: dict[str, Any], clauses: list[str]) -> str:
186	    """A schema-constrained per-source judging prompt over ALL pending clauses.
187	
188	    The model NEVER emits spans/offsets (deterministic owns those — deletes the
189	    "span must be an object" failure class). It returns ONLY a per-clause verdict +
190	    a one-line basis. UNKNOWN is a first-class, ENCOURAGED answer when the metadata
191	    does not decide the clause (so the caller's fail-open contract is honoured
192	    instead of a fabricated pass/fail).
193	    """
194	    numbered = "\n".join(
195	        f'  {i}. "{c}"' for i, c in enumerate(clauses)
196	    )
197	    meta_json = json.dumps(meta, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str)
198	    return (
199	        "You are a SOURCE-ELIGIBILITY JUDGE for a research pipeline. A user pinned "
200	        "hard sourcing constraints. Your job: for ONE fetched source, decide whether "
201	        "it SATISFIES each constraint clause, reading ONLY the metadata below.\n\n"
202	        "SOURCE METADATA (already fetched — do not assume anything not shown):\n"
203	        f"{meta_json}\n\n"
204	        "CONSTRAINT CLAUSES (verbatim from the user; each is a HARD requirement the "
205	        "source must satisfy to be citable):\n"
206	        f"{numbered}\n\n"
207	        "For EACH clause, decide:\n"
208	        '  - "pass": the source clearly SATISFIES the clause (e.g. the clause says '
209	        '"only company press releases" and this source IS a company press release; '
210	        'or "high-quality journal articles" and this is a peer-reviewed journal).\n'
211	        '  - "fail": the source clearly VIOLATES the clause (e.g. it is a personal '
212	        "blog when the clause demands news + press releases; or a predatory/OJS-mill "
213	        "venue when the clause demands high quality).\n"
214	        '  - "unknown": the metadata genuinely does not let you decide. Prefer this '
215	        "over guessing. Do NOT fabricate a verdict.\n\n"
216	        "Judge what the source ACTUALLY IS from title/venue/host/type/body — not what "
217	        "it is shaped like. A journal-SHAPED predatory venue is NOT high quality. A "
218	        "corporate newsroom page IS a company press release. A vendor/analyst PDF may "
219	        "be an industry white paper.\n\n"
220	        "Return ONLY a JSON object, no prose, of this exact shape:\n"
221	        '{"verdicts": [{"clause_index": <int>, "verdict": "pass|fail|unknown", '
222	        '"basis": "<one short sentence>"}, ...]}\n'
223	        "Include exactly one entry per clause index above."
224	    )
225	
226	
227	def _parse_judge_response(
228	    text: str, clauses: list[str]
229	) -> dict[int, tuple[str, str]]:
230	    """Parse the judge's JSON into ``{clause_index: (verdict, basis)}``.
231	
232	    Robust + per-item (Kimi's malformed-output contract): a missing/garbled entry
233	    for a clause maps to ``UNKNOWN`` (fail-open at the caller for opaque), NEVER a
234	    silent pass and never a crash. An out-of-range/unknown verdict token is coerced
235	    to ``UNKNOWN`` too.
236	    """
237	    out: dict[int, tuple[str, str]] = {}
238	    payload: Any = None
239	    raw = (text or "").strip()
240	    if raw:
241	        try:
242	            payload = json.loads(raw)
243	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — tolerate fenced / trailing-prose wrappers
244	            m = re.search(r"\{.*\}", raw, re.DOTALL)
245	            if m:
246	                try:
247	                    payload = json.loads(m.group(0))
248	                except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
249	                    payload = None
250	    verdicts = []
251	    if isinstance(payload, dict):
252	        verdicts = payload.get("verdicts") or []
253	    if isinstance(verdicts, list):
254	        for item in verdicts:
255	            if not isinstance(item, dict):
256	                continue
257	            try:
258	                idx = int(item.get("clause_index"))
259	            except (TypeError, ValueError):
260	                continue
261	            if not (0 <= idx < len(clauses)):
262	                continue
263	            v = str(item.get("verdict", "")).strip().lower()
264	            if v not in _LLM_VERDICTS:
265	                v = UNKNOWN
266	            basis = str(item.get("basis", "") or "").strip()[:280]
267	            out[idx] = (v, basis or "llm judge (no basis given)")
268	    # Any clause the model omitted -> UNKNOWN (fail-open at the caller for opaque).
269	    for i in range(len(clauses)):
270	        out.setdefault(i, (UNKNOWN, "llm judge returned no verdict for this clause"))
271	    return out
272	
273	
274	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
275	# judge_source — the per-source entry point (spec item 1)
276	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
277	
278	
279	# An LLM judge callable: (source_metadata_view, [clauses]) -> raw JSON response str.
280	LLMJudge = Callable[[dict[str, Any], list[str]], str]
281	
282	
283	def judge_source(
284	    source: Any,
285	    hard_clauses: list[str],
286	    *,
287	    llm: Optional[LLMJudge] = None,
288	    contract_hash: str = "",
289	) -> list[SourceReceipt]:
290	    """Judge ONE fetched source against a list of verbatim hard opaque clauses.
291	
292	    Layered (spec item 1): (a) a DETERMINISTIC predicate per clause first (named-host
293	    identity), high precision, no LLM; (b) for every clause the deterministic leg
294	    could NOT decide, ONE bounded schema-constrained LLM call scoring ALL of them at
295	    once (quality / topicality / opaque kinds).
296	
297	    Returns one :class:`SourceReceipt` per (source, clause) with
298	    ``stage=STAGE_OPAQUE``, ``term_id=opaque:<clause slug>``, and the verdict + basis.
299	    When ``llm`` is ``None`` and a clause needs the LLM leg, that clause resolves to
300	    ``UNKNOWN`` with a disclosed basis (never a fabricated pass — no-silent-fallback).
301	    """
302	    url = _row_url(source)
303	    receipts: list[SourceReceipt] = []
304	    clauses = [c for c in (hard_clauses or []) if str(c).strip()]
305	    if not url or not clauses:
306	        return receipts
307	
308	    pending: list[str] = []          # clauses left for the LLM leg
309	    resolved: dict[str, tuple[str, str]] = {}  # clause -> (verdict, basis)
310	
311	    # (a) deterministic leg — per clause.
312	    for clause in clauses:
313	        det = _deterministic_verdict(clause, source)
314	        if det is not None:
315	            resolved[clause] = det
316	        else:
317	            pending.append(clause)
318	
319	    # (b) LLM leg — one call over all still-pending clauses.
320	    if pending:
321	        if llm is not None:
322	            meta = _source_metadata_view(source)
323	            try:
324	                raw = llm(meta, pending)
325	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a judge fault is UNKNOWN, never a crash
326	                raw = ""
327	                for clause in pending:
328	                    resolved[clause] = (UNKNOWN, f"llm judge raised: {exc!r} (fail-open)")
329	            if raw is not None and any(c not in resolved for c in pending):
330	                parsed = _parse_judge_response(raw, pending)
331	                for i, clause in enumerate(pending):
332	                    if clause not in resolved:
333	                        resolved[clause] = parsed.get(
334	                            i, (UNKNOWN, "llm judge returned no verdict")
335	                        )
336	        else:
337	            # No judge injected: the clause is honestly undecided here (disclosed).
338	            for clause in pending:
339	                resolved[clause] = (
340	                    UNKNOWN,
341	                    "no LLM judge available; opaque clause left undecided (disclosed)",
342	                )
343	
344	    for clause in clauses:
345	        verdict, basis = resolved.get(clause, (UNKNOWN, "unresolved"))
346	        receipts.append(SourceReceipt(
347	            contract_hash=contract_hash,
348	            term_id=_clause_id(clause),
349	            source_id=url,
350	            stage=STAGE_OPAQUE,
351	            verdict=verdict,
352	            basis=f"{basis} | clause={clause!r}",
353	        ))
354	    return receipts
355	
356	
357	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
358	# build_opaque_eligibility — the aggregation into a citable-eligibility plan
359	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
360	
361	
362	@dataclass
363	class OpaquePlan:
364	    """The opaque-eligibility outcome over one billed candidate set.
365	
366	    Mirrors :class:`~src.polaris_graph.retrieval.quality_eligibility.EligibilityPlan`'s
367	    fields so the seam merges it with zero new plumbing: an
368	    ``eligibility_excluded_ids`` set the seam masks out of the citable menu, a demote
369	    weight map for SOFT clauses, per-source receipts, and a disclosure record list.
370	    """
371	
372	    eligibility_excluded_ids: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
373	    url_to_weight: dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=dict)
374	    receipts: list[SourceReceipt] = field(default_factory=list)
375	    excluded_records: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
376	
377	    def is_empty(self) -> bool:
378	        return not (
379	            self.eligibility_excluded_ids
380	            or self.url_to_weight
381	            or self.receipts
382	        )
383	
384	
385	def build_opaque_eligibility(
386	    policy: Any,
387	    evidence_rows: "list[Any] | None",
388	    *,
389	    llm: Optional[LLMJudge] = None,
390	    fail_open_on_unknown: bool = True,
391	) -> OpaquePlan:
392	    """Apply the contract's OPAQUE hard clauses to a billed candidate set.
393	
394	    ``policy`` is a :class:`RetrievalPolicy` (duck-typed: reads ``opaque_eligibility``,
395	    ``predicate_force['opaque_eligibility']``, ``contract_hash``). When no opaque
396	    clause is present, returns an EMPTY plan (no-op, byte-identical).
397	
398	    HARD clauses: a source that ``fail``s ANY hard opaque clause is added to
399	    ``eligibility_excluded_ids`` (removed from the citable menu, KEPT in corpus +
400	    diagnostics). ``unknown`` under a hard opaque clause is **fail-OPEN** by default
401	    (``fail_open_on_unknown=True``) — an opaque deontic clause the judge cannot decide
402	    must NOT silently quarantine a source (no-silent-fallback; matches topicality's
403	    fail-open). Set ``fail_open_on_unknown=False`` for a strict "only ..." clause the
404	    operator wants fail-closed. SOFT clauses: never exclude — a ``fail`` only demotes
405	    ``url_to_weight`` (order-not-drop).
406	    """
407	    plan = OpaquePlan()
408	    clauses = [str(c).strip() for c in (getattr(policy, "opaque_eligibility", None) or []) if str(c).strip()]
409	    if not clauses:
410	        return plan  # no opaque predicate -> byte-identical no-op
411	
412	    force = (getattr(policy, "predicate_force", {}) or {}).get("opaque_eligibility", "hard")
413	    is_hard = str(force).strip().lower() == "hard"
414	    contract_hash = str(getattr(policy, "contract_hash", "") or "")
415	
416	    for row in list(evidence_rows or []):
417	        url = _row_url(row)
418	        if not url:
419	            continue
420	        receipts = judge_source(
421	            row, clauses, llm=llm, contract_hash=contract_hash,
422	        )
423	        plan.receipts.extend(receipts)
424	        # Aggregate: a source is CITABLE only if it passes EVERY hard clause.
425	        row_failed = False
426	        row_min_weight = 1.0
427	        fail_basis = ""
428	        for rec in receipts:
429	            if rec.verdict == FAIL:
430	                row_failed = True
431	                fail_basis = rec.basis
432	                break
433	            if rec.verdict == UNKNOWN and not fail_open_on_unknown:
434	                # fail-closed variant: unknown under a strict "only ..." excludes.
435	                row_failed = True
436	                fail_basis = rec.basis
437	                break
438	            if rec.verdict == UNKNOWN:
439	                row_min_weight = min(row_min_weight, 0.5)
440	        if row_failed:
441	            if is_hard:
442	                plan.eligibility_excluded_ids.add(url)
443	                plan.excluded_records.append({
444	                    "source_id": url, "stage": STAGE_OPAQUE,
445	                    "verdict": FAIL, "basis": fail_basis, "force": "hard",
446	                })
447	            else:
448	                plan.url_to_weight[url] = 0.2
449	        elif row_min_weight < 1.0 and not is_hard:
450	            plan.url_to_weight[url] = row_min_weight
451	    return plan
452

1	"""Contract compliance audit (S4) — DISCLOSURE-ONLY, offline + deterministic.
2	
3	The gate's final read of its own pinned contract against the produced report
4	(consolidated design §6, Sol §4 compliance-audit row). It reports, for every
5	binding contract term, whether the deliverable SATISFIED / FAILED / left it
6	UNSATISFIABLE / UNKNOWN, and names the OWNING stage — so a forensic reader can
7	trace a shortfall to retrieval / outline / compose / render.
8	
9	Hard boundaries (guardrails)
10	----------------------------
11	* **DISCLOSURE-ONLY.** :func:`audit_contract` NEVER drops or edits a sentence,
12	  NEVER reorders/removes a section, NEVER touches ``strict_verify`` or the
13	  citation audit. It reads the finished report + outline + bibliography and
14	  returns a plain-data audit. It is called AFTER assembly, ALONGSIDE (never
15	  in place of) ``_audit_citations``. Its verdict cannot change the report.
16	* **Deterministic where it counts.** Section presence/order, requested item
17	  COUNTS, length range, table field availability, and citation presence are all
18	  decided by DETERMINISTIC string/number checks over the report + biblio — no
19	  model. A model judge is used ONLY for semantic topic coverage, and ONLY when a
20	  judge callable is explicitly injected (default ``None`` => that dimension is
21	  reported ``UNKNOWN``, never fabricated as satisfied). The judge can never alter
22	  a deterministic verdict and never touches faithfulness.
23	* **Faithfulness untouched.** This module imports nothing from
24	  ``provenance_generator`` and runs no verification pass. A term whose evidence
25	  simply did not survive faithfulness is reported FAILED/UNKNOWN with its owning
26	  stage — never re-litigated.
27	* **Never invent a requirement.** Only ``force == hard`` terms (schema-guaranteed
28	  explicit / user-backed) are audited as REQUIREMENTS. A preference/open term is
29	  reported as ``NOT_APPLICABLE`` (informational) — its absence is never a FAIL.
30	* **Fail-open.** A ``None`` / degraded / shapeless contract yields an empty audit
31	  (no findings), so a caller can always run it safely alongside the citation
32	  audit without risk to the deliverable.
33	"""
34	
35	from __future__ import annotations
36	
37	import re
38	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
39	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
40	
41	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
42	# Result vocabulary
43	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
44	
45	SATISFIED = "SATISFIED"
46	FAILED = "FAILED"
47	UNSATISFIABLE = "UNSATISFIABLE"
48	UNKNOWN = "UNKNOWN"
49	NOT_APPLICABLE = "NOT_APPLICABLE"
50	
51	STATUSES: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
52	    SATISFIED, FAILED, UNSATISFIABLE, UNKNOWN, NOT_APPLICABLE,
53	})
54	
55	# Which stage OWNS a shortfall on a given dimension (design §6 routing).
56	_STAGE_RETRIEVAL = "retrieval"
57	_STAGE_OUTLINE = "outline"
58	_STAGE_COMPOSE = "compose"
59	_STAGE_RENDER = "render"
60	_STAGE_AUDIT = "audit"
61	
62	
63	def _norm(s: Any) -> str:
64	    return str(s or "").strip()
65	
66	
67	def _term_value_text(term: Any) -> str:
68	    if term is None:
69	        return ""
70	    val = getattr(term, "value", term)
71	    if isinstance(val, (list, tuple)):
72	        return ", ".join(_norm(v) for v in val if _norm(v)).strip()
73	    return _norm(val)
74	
75	
76	def _is_hard(term: Any) -> bool:
77	    force = getattr(term, "force", None)
78	    if force is not None:
79	        return str(force).strip().lower() == "hard"
80	    if isinstance(term, dict):
81	        return str(term.get("force", "")).strip().lower() == "hard"
82	    return False

90	@dataclass
91	class ComplianceFinding:
92	    """One term-level compliance result routed to its owning stage."""
93	
94	    term_id: str
95	    dimension: str
96	    status: str            # one of STATUSES
97	    owning_stage: str      # retrieval | outline | compose | render | audit
98	    method: str            # "deterministic" | "judge" | "not_evaluated"
99	    detail: str = ""
100	    force: str = ""        # hard | preference | open (informational)
101	
102	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
103	        return {
104	            "term_id": self.term_id,
105	            "dimension": self.dimension,
106	            "status": self.status,
107	            "owning_stage": self.owning_stage,
108	            "method": self.method,
109	            "detail": self.detail,
110	            "force": self.force,
111	        }
112	
113	
114	@dataclass
115	class ComplianceAudit:
116	    """The full term-level audit. Disclosure-only; carries no drop/edit power."""
117	
118	    findings: list[ComplianceFinding] = field(default_factory=list)
119	    retrieval_scope_status: str = "not_evaluated_prebuilt_corpus"
120	    contract_sha256: str = ""
121	
122	    def counts(self) -> dict[str, int]:
123	        out: dict[str, int] = {s: 0 for s in STATUSES}
124	        for f in self.findings:
125	            out[f.status] = out.get(f.status, 0) + 1
126	        return out
127	
128	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
129	        return {
130	            "retrieval_scope_status": self.retrieval_scope_status,
131	            "contract_sha256": self.contract_sha256,
132	            "counts": self.counts(),
133	            "findings": [f.to_dict() for f in self.findings],
134	        }

141	
142	def audit_contract(
143	    contract: Any,
144	    report: str,
145	    outline: Any = None,
146	    biblio: Any = None,
147	    *,
148	    coverage_judge: Optional[Callable[[str, str], bool]] = None,
149	    retrieval_scope_status: str = "not_evaluated_prebuilt_corpus",
150	    contract_sha256: str = "",
151	    scope_receipts: Optional[list[Any]] = None,
152	) -> ComplianceAudit:
153	    """Audit a produced report against its pinned contract. DISCLOSURE-ONLY.
154	
155	    Parameters
156	    ----------
157	    contract:
158	        A pinned :class:`ResearchContract` (or duck-typed equivalent). ``None`` /
159	        degraded => an empty audit (fail-open).
160	    report:
161	        The final assembled report text (verified prose + bibliography).
162	    outline:
163	        The resolved outline — a list of section objects/dicts (``title`` attr /
164	        key) or plain title strings. Used for the deterministic section-order
165	        check. ``None`` => the report headings are parsed from ``report`` instead.
166	    biblio:
167	        The bibliography (list of dict rows) — used for citation-presence and
168	        references-dedup checks. ``None`` => parsed from the report's References.
169	    coverage_judge:
170	        OPTIONAL semantic-coverage judge ``(topic, report) -> bool``. When
171	        ``None`` (default), semantic topic coverage is reported ``UNKNOWN`` — it is
172	        NEVER fabricated as satisfied. The judge is a SEPARATE cheap model; it can
173	        never change a deterministic verdict and never touches faithfulness.
174	    retrieval_scope_status:
175	        Recorded verbatim. On a prebuilt corpus the caller passes
176	        ``"not_evaluated_prebuilt_corpus"`` (the default) so the audit never
177	        claims a retrieval-scope requirement was enforced when discovery never ran
178	        under the contract.
179	    scope_receipts:
180	        OPTIONAL per-(source, hard term, stage) eligibility receipts (duck-typed:
181	        ``term_id`` / ``verdict`` in {"pass","fail","unknown"}). When present, a hard
182	        retrieval-scope term is SATISFIED only if its executable stage RAN and every
183	        citable source subject to it carries a ``pass`` receipt; a single ``fail``
184	        is FAILED; any ``unknown`` (or no receipt for the term) stays UNKNOWN — a
185	        caller-supplied status string can NEVER promote UNKNOWN (Sol §4). This is
186	        the wire that makes quality / topicality / date / source-type auditable
187	        instead of decorative — it reads receipts, never re-verifies a claim.
188	
189	    Returns
190	    -------
191	    A :class:`ComplianceAudit` with one :class:`ComplianceFinding` per audited
192	    term. Deterministic for section-order / counts / length / tables / citations;
193	    a single judge call per semantic topic ONLY when a judge is injected.
194	    """
195	    audit = ComplianceAudit(
196	        retrieval_scope_status=_norm(retrieval_scope_status)
197	        or "not_evaluated_prebuilt_corpus",
198	        contract_sha256=_norm(contract_sha256),
199	    )
200	    if contract is None or not isinstance(report, str):
201	        return audit
202	
203	    report_text = report or ""
204	    report_headings = _report_headings(report_text)
205	    outline_titles = _outline_titles(outline) or report_headings
206	    biblio_rows = _biblio_rows(biblio, report_text)
207	
208	    # 1) Required sections + order (RENDER-owned, deterministic).
209	    audit.findings.extend(
210	        _audit_sections(contract, outline_titles)
211	    )
212	    # 2) Requested item counts (COMPOSE-owned, deterministic).
213	    audit.findings.extend(
214	        _audit_counts(contract, report_text)
215	    )
216	    # 3) Length range (RENDER-owned, deterministic — DISCLOSURE, never a cap).
217	    audit.findings.extend(
218	        _audit_length(contract, report_text)
219	    )
220	    # 4) Table / visual field availability (RENDER-owned, deterministic).
221	    audit.findings.extend(
222	        _audit_visuals(contract, report_text)
223	    )
224	    # 5) Citations present (RENDER-owned, deterministic).
225	    audit.findings.extend(
226	        _audit_citations_present(contract, report_text, biblio_rows)
227	    )
228	    # 6) Retrieval-scope hard terms (RETRIEVAL-owned): on a prebuilt corpus these
229	    #    are UNKNOWN (discovery never ran under the contract) — disclosed, never
230	    #    claimed satisfied.
231	    audit.findings.extend(
232	        _audit_retrieval_scope(
233	            contract, audit.retrieval_scope_status,
234	            receipts=scope_receipts,
235	        )
236	    )
237	    # 7) Semantic topic coverage (COMPOSE/OUTLINE-owned): deterministic keyword
238	    #    presence is a HINT; the authoritative call is the injected judge. No
239	    #    judge => UNKNOWN (never fabricated).
240	    audit.findings.extend(
241	        _audit_coverage(contract, report_text, coverage_judge)
242	    )
243	
244	    return audit
245

248	# Deterministic sub-audits

251	_HEADING_RE = re.compile(r"^#{1,6}\s+(.*?)\s*$", re.MULTILINE)
252	
253	
254	def _report_headings(report: str) -> list[str]:
255	    return [h.strip() for h in _HEADING_RE.findall(report or "") if h.strip()]
256	
257	
258	def _outline_titles(outline: Any) -> list[str]:
259	    if not isinstance(outline, (list, tuple)):
260	        return []
261	    out: list[str] = []
262	    for item in outline:
263	        if isinstance(item, str):
264	            t = _norm(item)
265	        elif isinstance(item, dict):
266	            t = _norm(item.get("title"))
267	        else:
268	            t = _norm(getattr(item, "title", ""))
269	        if t:
270	            out.append(t)
271	    return out

274	def _biblio_rows(biblio: Any, report: str) -> list[dict]:
275	    if isinstance(biblio, (list, tuple)):
276	        return [b for b in biblio if isinstance(b, dict)]
277	    # parse from the report's References block as a fallback.
278	    body = report.split("\n## References\n", 1)
279	    if len(body) < 2:
280	        return []
281	    rows: list[dict] = []
282	    for line in body[1].splitlines():
283	        m = re.match(r"\s*\[(\d+)\]\s+(.*)", line)
284	        if m:
285	            rows.append({"num": int(m.group(1)), "statement": m.group(2)})
286	    return rows

289	def _explicit_sections(contract: Any) -> list[Any]:
290	    """Sections with an exact-title lock (explicit/user), in contract order."""
291	    out: list[Any] = []
292	    sections = getattr(contract, "sections", None)
293	    if isinstance(sections, (list, tuple)):
294	        for sec in sections:
295	            if getattr(sec, "exact_title_lock", False) and _term_value_text(
296	                getattr(sec, "title", None)
297	            ):
298	                out.append(sec)
299	    return out

301	
302	def _title_present(title: str, produced: list[str]) -> bool:
303	    """Case-insensitive presence: an exact heading OR a heading containing it."""
304	    tl = title.strip().lower()
305	    if not tl:
306	        return False
307	    for p in produced:
308	        pl = p.strip().lower()
309	        if pl == tl or tl in pl or pl in tl:
310	            return True
311	    return False
312	
313	
314	def _audit_sections(contract: Any, produced_titles: list[str]) -> list[ComplianceFinding]:
315	    findings: list[ComplianceFinding] = []
316	    explicit = _explicit_sections(contract)
317	    if not explicit:
318	        return findings
319	
320	    # presence
321	    ordered_required: list[tuple[Optional[int], str, str]] = []
322	    for sec in explicit:
323	        title = _term_value_text(getattr(sec, "title", None))
324	        tid = _norm(getattr(getattr(sec, "title", None), "term_id", "")) or _norm(
325	            getattr(sec, "section_id", "")
326	        )
327	        order = getattr(sec, "order", None)
328	        present = _title_present(title, produced_titles)
329	        findings.append(ComplianceFinding(
330	            term_id=tid,
331	            dimension="deliverable.section",
332	            status=SATISFIED if present else FAILED,
333	            owning_stage=_STAGE_RENDER,
334	            method="deterministic",
335	            detail=(f"required section {title!r} "
336	                    + ("present" if present else "absent from produced headings")),
337	            force="hard",
338	        ))
339	        ordered_required.append((order if isinstance(order, int) else None, title, tid))
340	
341	    # order: only when at least two required sections carry explicit orders.
342	    with_order = [(o, t, tid) for (o, t, tid) in ordered_required if o is not None]
343	    if len(with_order) >= 2:
344	        want = [t for _, t, _ in sorted(with_order, key=lambda x: x[0])]
345	        # positions of each required title in the produced list.
346	        positions: list[tuple[str, int]] = []
347	        low_titles = [p.strip().lower() for p in produced_titles]
348	        for t in want:
349	            idx = next(
350	                (i for i, pl in enumerate(low_titles)
351	                 if pl == t.lower() or t.lower() in pl or pl in t.lower()),
352	                -1,
353	            )
354	            positions.append((t, idx))
355	        found = [(t, i) for t, i in positions if i >= 0]
356	        in_order = all(
357	            found[k][1] <= found[k + 1][1] for k in range(len(found) - 1)
358	        ) if len(found) >= 2 else True
359	        findings.append(ComplianceFinding(
360	            term_id="deliverable.section_order",
361	            dimension="deliverable.section_order",
362	            status=SATISFIED if (len(found) == len(want) and in_order) else FAILED,
363	            owning_stage=_STAGE_RENDER,
364	            method="deterministic",
365	            detail=(f"required order {want!r}; produced positions "
366	                    f"{[(t, i) for t, i in positions]!r}"),
367	            force="hard",
368	        ))
369	    return findings
370

372	_NUM_WORDS = {
373	    "one": 1, "two": 2, "three": 3, "four": 4, "five": 5, "six": 6,
374	    "seven": 7, "eight": 8, "nine": 9, "ten": 10, "eleven": 11, "twelve": 12,
375	}

377	
378	def _requested_count(term: Any) -> Optional[int]:
379	    """Extract a requested item count from a term value/dimension (or None)."""
380	    val = getattr(term, "value", term)
381	    if isinstance(val, bool):
382	        return None
383	    if isinstance(val, int):
384	        return val
385	    txt = _term_value_text(term).lower()
386	    if not txt:
387	        return None
388	    m = re.search(r"\b(\d+)\b", txt)
389	    if m:
390	        try:
391	            return int(m.group(1))
392	        except ValueError:
393	            return None
394	    for w, n in _NUM_WORDS.items():
395	        if re.search(rf"\b{w}\b", txt):
396	            return n
397	    return None
398	
399	
400	def _audit_counts(contract: Any, report: str) -> list[ComplianceFinding]:
401	    """Requested item counts (e.g. 'top ten') as a deterministic disclosure.
402	
403	    A count check counts DISTINCT numbered list markers (``1.`` / ``- ``) in the
404	    report as a lower bound. This is a DISCLOSURE hint: too few is FAILED, enough
405	    is SATISFIED. It never edits the report.
406	    """
407	    findings: list[ComplianceFinding] = []
408	    coverage = getattr(contract, "coverage", None)
409	    if not isinstance(coverage, (list, tuple)):
410	        return findings
411	    # count numbered list items in the report (a lower-bound signal).
412	    numbered = len(re.findall(r"^\s*\d+[.)]\s+", report, re.MULTILINE))
413	    for cr in coverage:
414	        stmt = getattr(cr, "statement", None)
415	        if not _is_hard(stmt):
416	            continue
417	        want = _requested_count(stmt)
418	        if want is None:
419	            continue
420	        tid = _norm(getattr(stmt, "term_id", "")) or _norm(
421	            getattr(cr, "requirement_id", "")
422	        )
423	        ok = numbered >= want
424	        findings.append(ComplianceFinding(
425	            term_id=tid,
426	            dimension="content.count",
427	            status=SATISFIED if ok else UNKNOWN,
428	            owning_stage=_STAGE_COMPOSE,
429	            method="deterministic",
430	            detail=(f"requested count {want}; numbered items found {numbered} "
431	                    + ("(>= requested)" if ok else "(fewer numbered markers — "
432	                       "may be prose-listed; disclosed as UNKNOWN, not FAILED)")),
433	            force="hard",
434	        ))
435	    return findings
436	
437	
438	def _audit_length(contract: Any, report: str) -> list[ComplianceFinding]:
439	    """Length terms as DISCLOSURE (never a cap). min/target/max compared to the
440	    report word count; a min not met is FAILED, otherwise SATISFIED."""
441	    findings: list[ComplianceFinding] = []
442	    deliverable = getattr(contract, "deliverable", None)
443	    if not isinstance(deliverable, (list, tuple)):
444	        return findings
445	    words = len((report or "").split())
446	    for t in deliverable:
447	        dim = _norm(getattr(t, "dimension", "")).lower()
448	        if "length" not in dim:
449	            continue
450	        if not _is_hard(t):
451	            continue
452	        want = _requested_count(t)
453	        if want is None:
454	            continue
455	        # a "minimum"/"at least" reading fails when under; otherwise it is a
456	        # target/max and is disclosed as SATISFIED (length is never truncated).
457	        is_min = "min" in dim or "at least" in _term_value_text(t).lower()
458	        status = FAILED if (is_min and words < want) else SATISFIED
459	        findings.append(ComplianceFinding(
460	            term_id=_norm(getattr(t, "term_id", "")) or dim,
461	            dimension="deliverable.length",
462	            status=status,
463	            owning_stage=_STAGE_RENDER,
464	            method="deterministic",
465	            detail=f"length term {dim}={want}; report words={words} "
466	                   f"(length is disclosure, never a truncation gate)",
467	            force="hard",
468	        ))
469	    return findings
470

472	def _audit_visuals(contract: Any, report: str) -> list[ComplianceFinding]:
473	    """Requested tables/visuals: SATISFIED iff a markdown table (or the named
474	    visual keyword) is present. Deterministic; RENDER-owned."""
475	    findings: list[ComplianceFinding] = []
476	    has_table = bool(re.search(r"^\s*\|.*\|\s*$", report, re.MULTILINE))
477	    for group_name in ("deliverable", "content_terms"):
478	        group = getattr(contract, group_name, None)
479	        if not isinstance(group, (list, tuple)):
480	            continue
481	        for t in group:
482	            dim = _norm(getattr(t, "dimension", "")).lower()
483	            if not any(k in dim for k in ("visual", "table", "figure", "chart", "timeline")):
484	                continue
485	            if not _is_hard(t):
486	                continue
487	            wants_table = "table" in dim or "table" in _term_value_text(t).lower()
488	            present = has_table if wants_table else _visual_keyword_present(t, report)
489	            findings.append(ComplianceFinding(
490	                term_id=_norm(getattr(t, "term_id", "")) or dim,
491	                dimension="deliverable.visual",
492	                status=SATISFIED if present else FAILED,
493	                owning_stage=_STAGE_RENDER,
494	                method="deterministic",
495	                detail=(f"requested visual {dim!r}; "
496	                        + ("present" if present else "not found in report "
497	                           "(render only builds visuals from VERIFIED fields)")),
498	                force="hard",
499	            ))

503	def _visual_keyword_present(term: Any, report: str) -> bool:
504	    kw = _term_value_text(term).lower()
505	    rl = report.lower()
506	    for token in ("table", "figure", "chart", "timeline", "mind map", "matrix"):
507	        if token in kw and token in rl:

512	def _audit_citations_present(
513	    contract: Any, report: str, biblio_rows: list[dict]
514	) -> list[ComplianceFinding]:
515	    """Citation requirement: SATISFIED iff the report carries [N] markers AND a
516	    bibliography. Deterministic; RENDER-owned. Never touches _audit_citations."""
517	    findings: list[ComplianceFinding] = []
518	    for group_name in ("deliverable",):
519	        group = getattr(contract, group_name, None)
520	        if not isinstance(group, (list, tuple)):
521	            continue
522	        for t in group:
523	            dim = _norm(getattr(t, "dimension", "")).lower()
524	            if "citation" not in dim and "bibliograph" not in dim:
525	                continue
526	            if not _is_hard(t):
527	                continue
528	            has_markers = bool(re.search(r"\[\d+\]", report))
529	            has_biblio = len(biblio_rows) > 0
530	            ok = has_markers and has_biblio
531	            findings.append(ComplianceFinding(
532	                term_id=_norm(getattr(t, "term_id", "")) or dim,
533	                dimension="deliverable.citation",
534	                status=SATISFIED if ok else FAILED,
535	                owning_stage=_STAGE_RENDER,
536	                method="deterministic",
537	                detail=f"citation markers={has_markers}, bibliography rows="
538	                       f"{len(biblio_rows)}",
539	                force="hard",
540	            ))
541	    return findings

543	
544	def _receipt_verdict_for_term(
545	    term_id: str, receipts: Optional[list[Any]]
546	) -> tuple[Optional[str], str]:
547	    """Fold this term's per-source eligibility receipts into ONE audit verdict.
548	
549	    Returns ``(status, detail)`` where ``status`` is:
550	      * ``SATISFIED`` — the stage RAN and EVERY citable source subject to the term
551	        passed (>=1 pass, 0 fail, 0 unknown);
552	      * ``FAILED`` — at least one source FAILED the hard predicate;
553	      * ``UNKNOWN`` — the stage ran but a source's verdict was unknown (fail-closed:
554	        never fabricated as satisfied);
555	      * ``None`` — NO receipt for this term (the caller keeps the prebuilt-corpus
556	        UNKNOWN so a caller-supplied status string can never promote it).
557	    """
558	    if not receipts:
559	        return None, ""
560	    mine = [r for r in receipts
561	            if str(_receipt_get(r, "term_id")).strip() == term_id]
562	    if not mine:
563	        return None, ""
564	    verdicts = [str(_receipt_get(r, "verdict")).strip().lower() for r in mine]
565	    n_pass = verdicts.count("pass")
566	    n_fail = verdicts.count("fail")
567	    n_unknown = verdicts.count("unknown")
568	    if n_fail:
569	        return FAILED, (f"{n_fail} source(s) FAILED the hard predicate "
570	                        f"(pass={n_pass}, unknown={n_unknown}) — receipt-backed")
571	    if n_unknown:
572	        return UNKNOWN, (f"{n_unknown} source(s) UNKNOWN under the hard predicate "
573	                         f"(pass={n_pass}) — fail-closed, not claimed satisfied")
574	    if n_pass:
575	        return SATISFIED, (f"all {n_pass} citable source(s) PASSED the hard "
576	                           "predicate — receipt-backed")
577	    return None, ""
578	
579	
580	def _receipt_get(r: Any, key: str) -> Any:
581	    if isinstance(r, dict):
582	        return r.get(key, "")
583	    return getattr(r, key, "")
584	
585	
586	def _audit_retrieval_scope(
587	    contract: Any,
588	    retrieval_scope_status: str,
589	    *,
590	    receipts: Optional[list[Any]] = None,
591	) -> list[ComplianceFinding]:
592	    """Hard SCOPE terms (source type / language / date / jurisdiction / quality /
593	    topicality). When per-source eligibility RECEIPTS are supplied, a term is
594	    SATISFIED only if its stage ran and every citable source passed; a fail is
595	    FAILED; an unknown stays UNKNOWN (fail-closed). Without receipts (prebuilt
596	    corpus) discovery never ran under the contract, so the term is disclosed
597	    UNKNOWN — never claimed satisfied. RETRIEVAL-owned."""
598	    findings: list[ComplianceFinding] = []
599	    scope = getattr(contract, "scope", None)
600	    if not isinstance(scope, (list, tuple)):
601	        return findings
602	    for t in scope:
603	        if not _is_hard(t):
604	            continue
605	        val = getattr(t, "value", None)
606	        if val in (None, "", [], {}):
607	            continue
608	        dim = _norm(getattr(t, "dimension", "")) or "scope"
609	        tid = _norm(getattr(t, "term_id", "")) or dim
610	        # Prefer a receipt-backed verdict (the executable stage RAN under this
611	        # contract); fall back to the disclosure-only UNKNOWN when no receipt
612	        # covers the term. A caller-supplied status string can NEVER promote UNKNOWN.
613	        r_status, r_detail = _receipt_verdict_for_term(tid, receipts)
614	        if r_status is None:
615	            # also try the bare dimension id (bridge stages key by dimension).
616	            r_status, r_detail = _receipt_verdict_for_term(dim, receipts)
617	        if r_status is not None:
618	            findings.append(ComplianceFinding(
619	                term_id=tid,
620	                dimension=dim,
621	                status=r_status,
622	                owning_stage=_STAGE_RETRIEVAL,
623	                method="receipt",
624	                detail=(f"hard scope {dim}={_term_value_text(t)!r}: {r_detail}"),
625	                force="hard",
626	            ))
627	            continue
628	        findings.append(ComplianceFinding(
629	            term_id=tid,
630	            dimension=dim,
631	            status=UNKNOWN,
632	            owning_stage=_STAGE_RETRIEVAL,
633	            method="not_evaluated",
634	            detail=(f"hard scope {dim}={_term_value_text(t)!r}; "
635	                    f"retrieval_scope_status={retrieval_scope_status} — "
636	                    "discovery not evaluated under this contract "
637	                    "(disclosed, not claimed satisfied)"),
638	            force="hard",
639	        ))
640	    return findings
641	
642	
643	def _audit_coverage(
644	    contract: Any,
645	    report: str,
646	    coverage_judge: Optional[Callable[[str, str], bool]],
647	) -> list[ComplianceFinding]:
648	    """Semantic topic coverage. The authoritative call is the injected judge; no
649	    judge => UNKNOWN (never fabricated). COMPOSE/OUTLINE-owned."""
650	    findings: list[ComplianceFinding] = []
651	    coverage = getattr(contract, "coverage", None)
652	    if not isinstance(coverage, (list, tuple)):
653	        return findings
654	    rl = (report or "").lower()
655	    for cr in coverage:
656	        if not getattr(cr, "required", True):
657	            continue
658	        stmt = getattr(cr, "statement", None)
659	        topic = _term_value_text(stmt)
660	        if not topic:
661	            continue
662	        tid = _norm(getattr(stmt, "term_id", "")) or _norm(
663	            getattr(cr, "requirement_id", "")
664	        )
665	        force = _norm(getattr(stmt, "force", "")) or "preference"
666	        if coverage_judge is not None:
667	            try:
668	                ok = bool(coverage_judge(topic, report))
669	                status = SATISFIED if ok else FAILED
670	                method = "judge"
671	                detail = f"semantic-coverage judge on topic {topic!r}: {ok}"
672	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — judge failure => UNKNOWN
673	                status = UNKNOWN
674	                method = "judge"
675	                detail = f"coverage judge errored on {topic!r}: {exc}"
676	        else:
677	            # deterministic keyword-presence HINT (never authoritative).
678	            hint = any(
679	                w and w in rl
680	                for w in re.findall(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z\-]{3,}", topic.lower())
681	            )
682	            status = UNKNOWN
683	            method = "not_evaluated"
684	            detail = (f"no semantic-coverage judge injected; keyword-presence "
685	                      f"hint={hint} for topic {topic!r} (reported UNKNOWN, "
686	                      "never fabricated as satisfied)")
687	        findings.append(ComplianceFinding(
688	            term_id=tid,
689	            dimension="content.coverage",
690	            status=status,
691	            owning_stage=_STAGE_COMPOSE if _is_hard(stmt) else _STAGE_OUTLINE,
692	            method=method,
693	            detail=detail,
694	            force=force,
695	        ))
696	    return findings
