1489	def validate_contract(contract: ResearchContract, prompt: str) -> list[ValidationError]:
1490	    """Deterministic contract validation. Returns a (possibly empty) error list.
1491	
1492	    NEVER raises for a correctness problem — it accumulates structured errors so
1493	    the compiler can run one bounded correction retry, and so tests can assert on
1494	    specific codes. The checks (design §3 / §4):
1495	
1496	    * ``hard_not_explicit`` — a ``force==hard`` term whose ``origin`` is not
1497	      explicit/user_answer/user_edit (the no-invention invariant).
1498	    * ``explicit_without_span`` — an ``origin==explicit`` term with no span.
1499	    * ``span_quote_mismatch`` — an explicit term's span where
1500	      ``prompt[start:end] != quote``.
1501	    * ``bad_origin`` / ``bad_force`` / ``bad_stage`` — out-of-vocab enum leaked in.
1502	    * ``inferred_not_disclosed`` — an inferred/policy_default term with a value
1503	      but no matching :class:`Assumption` (every inferred term must be disclosed).
1504	    * ``duplicate_term_id`` — two terms share a ``term_id`` (IDs must be stable
1505	      and unique for downstream projection).
1506	    """
1507	    errors: list[ValidationError] = []
1508	    prompt = prompt or ""
1509	
1510	    seen_ids: set[str] = set()
1511	    disclosed: set[str] = set()
1512	    for a in contract.assumptions:
1513	        disclosed.update(a.affected_term_ids)
1514	
1515	    for term in contract.all_terms():
1516	        tid = term.term_id or "<anon>"
1517	
1518	        if term.term_id:
1519	            if term.term_id in seen_ids:
1520	                errors.append(ValidationError(
1521	                    "duplicate_term_id",
1522	                    f"term_id {term.term_id!r} appears more than once",
1523	                    term_id=term.term_id,
1524	                ))
1525	            seen_ids.add(term.term_id)
1526	
1527	        if term.origin not in ORIGINS:
1528	            errors.append(ValidationError(
1529	                "bad_origin", f"unknown origin {term.origin!r}", term_id=tid))
1530	        if term.force not in FORCES:
1531	            errors.append(ValidationError(
1532	                "bad_force", f"unknown force {term.force!r}", term_id=tid))
1533	        for st in term.enforcement_stages:
1534	            if st not in STAGES:
1535	                errors.append(ValidationError(
1536	                    "bad_stage", f"unknown enforcement stage {st!r}", term_id=tid))
1537	
1538	        # -- generic-IR enum + operator/value schema (Phase B) --
1539	        if term.operator and term.operator not in OPERATORS:
1540	            errors.append(ValidationError(
1541	                "bad_operator", f"unknown operator {term.operator!r}", term_id=tid))
1542	        if term.stage_owner and term.stage_owner not in STAGE_OWNERS:
1543	            errors.append(ValidationError(
1544	                "bad_stage_owner",
1545	                f"unknown stage_owner {term.stage_owner!r}", term_id=tid))
1546	        if (
1547	            term.normalization_status
1548	            and term.normalization_status not in NORMALIZATION_STATUSES
1549	        ):
1550	            errors.append(ValidationError(
1551	                "bad_normalization_status",
1552	                f"unknown normalization_status {term.normalization_status!r}",
1553	                term_id=tid))
1554	        # IN / NOT_IN require a non-empty value_set; the scalar operators require a
1555	        # value. A hard set/bound with an empty payload can never be enforced.
1556	        if term.operator in (OP_IN, OP_NOT_IN) and not term.value_set:
1557	            errors.append(ValidationError(
1558	                "operator_value_mismatch",
1559	                f"term {tid!r} operator={term.operator} requires a non-empty value_set",
1560	                term_id=tid))
1561	        if (
1562	            term.operator in (OP_GTE, OP_LTE, OP_EQ, OP_BETWEEN)
1563	            and term.value in (None, "", [], {})
1564	        ):
1565	            errors.append(ValidationError(
1566	                "operator_value_mismatch",
1567	                f"term {tid!r} operator={term.operator} requires a value",
1568	                term_id=tid))
1569	
1570	        # THE no-invention invariant: hard requires an authoritative origin.
1571	        if term.is_hard() and term.origin not in HARD_ELIGIBLE_ORIGINS:
1572	            errors.append(ValidationError(
1573	                "hard_not_explicit",
1574	                f"term {tid!r} is force=hard but origin={term.origin!r}; a hard "
1575	                f"term requires origin in {sorted(HARD_ELIGIBLE_ORIGINS)}",
1576	                term_id=tid,
1577	            ))
1578	
1579	        # explicit ⇒ at least one span, and every span must quote-match.
1580	        if term.origin == ORIGIN_EXPLICIT:
1581	            if not term.spans:
1582	                errors.append(ValidationError(
1583	                    "explicit_without_span",
1584	                    f"term {tid!r} is origin=explicit but carries no prompt span",
1585	                    term_id=tid,
1586	                ))
1587	            for sp in term.spans:
1588	                if not sp.matches_prompt(prompt):
1589	                    errors.append(ValidationError(
1590	                        "span_quote_mismatch",
1591	                        f"term {tid!r} span quote != prompt[{sp.start}:{sp.end}]",
1592	                        term_id=tid,
1593	                        span=sp.to_dict(),
1594	                    ))
1595	
1596	        # No unanchored EXPLICIT-origin hard term: an origin==explicit hard term
1597	        # MUST carry at least one prompt span (the span checks above already fire
1598	        # for the missing/mismatched span; this makes the "hard needs an anchor"
1599	        # rule explicit for the non-explicit hard-eligible origins too — a
1600	        # user_answer/user_edit hard term is allowed WITHOUT a span, but an
1601	        # explicit hard term without a span is a no-invention violation).
1602	        if term.is_hard() and term.origin == ORIGIN_EXPLICIT and not term.spans:
1603	            errors.append(ValidationError(
1604	                "hard_explicit_unanchored",
1605	                f"hard explicit term {tid!r} has no prompt span to anchor it",
1606	                term_id=tid,
1607	            ))
1608	
1609	        # inferred/policy_default with a real value must be disclosed.
1610	        if (
1611	            term.origin in DISCLOSURE_ORIGINS
1612	            and term.value not in (None, "", [], {})
1613	            and term.term_id
1614	            and term.term_id not in disclosed
1615	        ):
1616	            errors.append(ValidationError(
1617	                "inferred_not_disclosed",
1618	                f"term {tid!r} is origin={term.origin!r} with a value but has no "
1619	                f"assumption record disclosing it",
1620	                term_id=tid,
1621	            ))
1622	
1623	    return errors
1624

1626	def validate_monotonicity(
1627	    contract: ResearchContract, candidate_ids: "set[str] | frozenset[str]"
1628	) -> list[ValidationError]:
1629	    """MONOTONICITY invariant (Phase B core): every deterministic explicit
1630	    candidate SURVIVED into the contract.
1631	
1632	    ``candidate_ids`` is the set of stable candidate term_ids the deterministic
1633	    core authored (see ``candidate_adapter.candidate_term_id``). If any one is
1634	    absent from ``contract.all_terms()``, the LLM (or a merge bug) dropped a
1635	    stated constraint — the exact inversion defect. This NEVER mutates; the
1636	    compiler runs it after the monotonic merge and treats a violation as fatal
1637	    (the deterministic term is then force-reinstated, and this is a belt).
1638	    """
1639	    present = {t.term_id for t in contract.all_terms() if t.term_id}
1640	    errors: list[ValidationError] = []
1641	    for cid in sorted(candidate_ids):
1642	        if cid and cid not in present:
1643	            errors.append(ValidationError(
1644	                "candidate_dropped",
1645	                f"deterministic explicit candidate {cid!r} did not survive into "
1646	                f"the contract (monotonic-lossless authority violated)",
1647	                term_id=cid,
1648	            ))
1649	    return errors

1663	
1664	
1665	def validate_plan(
1666	    plan: ResearchExecutionPlan, contract: ResearchContract
1667	) -> list[ValidationError]:
1668	    """Deterministic plan validation (design §4 plan-validation).
1669	
1670	    * ``requirement_without_intent`` — a required, evidence-bearing coverage
1671	      requirement with no mandatory query intent referencing it (via a thread).
1672	    * ``mandatory_lane_truncatable`` — ``budget.max_queries`` below
1673	      ``mandatory_lane_count`` under an ``expand``-or-``fail`` overflow policy is
1674	      only OK if the policy is not silent truncation; a smaller cap with a bad
1675	      policy is flagged.
1676	    * ``binding_term_unowned`` — a hard contract term with no coverage binding.
1677	    """
1678	    errors: list[ValidationError] = []
1679	
1680	    intent_ids_by_thread: dict[str, list[QueryIntent]] = {}
1681	    for qi in plan.query_intents:
1682	        intent_ids_by_thread.setdefault(qi.thread_id, []).append(qi)
1683	
1684	    covered_req_ids: set[str] = set()
1685	    for th in plan.threads:
1686	        has_mandatory_intent = any(
1687	            qi.mandatory for qi in intent_ids_by_thread.get(th.thread_id, [])
1688	        )
1689	        if has_mandatory_intent:
1690	            covered_req_ids.update(th.coverage_requirement_ids)
1691	
1692	    for cr in contract.coverage:
1693	        if not cr.required:
1694	            continue
1695	        if cr.requirement_id and cr.requirement_id not in covered_req_ids:
1696	            errors.append(ValidationError(
1697	                "requirement_without_intent",
1698	                f"required coverage {cr.requirement_id!r} has no mandatory query "
1699	                f"intent (via a thread)",
1700	                term_id=cr.requirement_id,
1701	            ))
1702	
1703	    bound_term_ids = {cb.contract_term_id for cb in plan.coverage_matrix}
1704	    for term in contract.hard_terms():
1705	        if term.term_id and term.term_id not in bound_term_ids:
1706	            errors.append(ValidationError(
1707	                "binding_term_unowned",
1708	                f"hard term {term.term_id!r} has no coverage binding / owning stage",
1709	                term_id=term.term_id,
1710	            ))
1711	
1712	    b = plan.budget
1713	    mandatory = sum(1 for qi in plan.query_intents if qi.mandatory)
1714	    if b.mandatory_lane_count and mandatory and b.mandatory_lane_count != mandatory:
1715	        # advisory: recorded lane count should match the actual mandatory intents
1716	        errors.append(ValidationError(
1717	            "mandatory_lane_count_mismatch",
1718	            f"budget.mandatory_lane_count={b.mandatory_lane_count} but "
1719	            f"{mandatory} mandatory query intents exist",
1720	        ))
1721	    if (
1722	        b.max_queries is not None
1723	        and b.mandatory_lane_count
1724	        and b.max_queries < b.mandatory_lane_count
1725	        and b.overflow_policy != "fail_preflight"
1726	    ):
1727	        errors.append(ValidationError(
1728	            "mandatory_lane_truncatable",
1729	            f"max_queries={b.max_queries} < mandatory_lane_count="
1730	            f"{b.mandatory_lane_count} under overflow_policy={b.overflow_policy!r}; "
1731	            f"a mandatory lane could be silently truncated",
1732	        ))
1733	
1734	    return errors
1735

1	"""Research Planning Gate compiler (S1) — contract + plan, OFFLINE-testable.
2	
3	Turns a raw task prompt into a pinned :class:`PlanningGateArtifact` via two
4	bounded structured LLM calls:
5	
6	  1. **contract compiler** — deterministic candidates (S0 adapter) + the raw
7	     prompt → a typed :class:`ResearchContract` (every clause dispositioned,
8	     every term tagged origin/force/spans);
9	  2. **plan compiler** — the *validated* contract → a
10	     :class:`ResearchExecutionPlan` (threads, mandatory query intents, outline
11	     seed, coverage matrix, budget).
12	
13	Both calls go through the repo LLM gateway (:class:`OpenRouterClient`) resolved
14	to the small policy model (``z-ai/glm-5.2``, the same arm FS query-gen uses).
15	The live call is OFF by default: the whole module is spend-free and hermetic
16	unless a flag is set OR a ``client=`` stub is injected (tests do the latter).
17	
18	Guardrail posture
19	-----------------
20	* **Default-OFF / None-default.** ``run_research_planning_gate`` requires an
21	  explicit ``client`` (a stub in tests) OR the live flag
22	  ``PG_PLANNING_GATE_LIVE=1``. With neither, and no client, it raises rather
23	  than silently hitting the network — the OFF path never fires an LLM.
24	* **Autonomous mode is pure ``contract→contract``.** It NEVER returns
25	  ``needs_input``, never blocks, never waits for a human: every would-ask becomes
26	  a disclosed :class:`Assumption` and the artifact is ``auto_pinned``. This is
27	  the load-bearing benchmark mode (a test asserts zero blocking with no input
28	  channel).
29	* **No invention.** The compiler system prompt forbids inventing constraints and
30	  requires spans for explicit terms; the deterministic
31	  :func:`~planning_gate_schema.validate_contract` then MECHANICALLY rejects any
32	  hard term that is not explicit/user-backed. A rejected contract gets ONE
33	  correction retry, then a conservative span-verified fallback
34	  (``compiler_degraded=true``) — never a guessed contract, never a crash.
35	* **Reuse.** Candidate seeding reuses the S0 ``candidate_adapter``; parsing
36	  reuses the fence-tolerant discipline of ``constraint_extractor`` /
37	  ``research_planner``; hashing reuses ``planning_gate_schema.sha256_of`` (same
38	  construction as ``research_planner.plan_sha256``).
39	
40	Nothing here is wired into the paid pipeline — that is S2+. This module is the
41	compiler + its offline harness.
42	"""
43

102	# the pinned contract, distinct from the interactive/autonomous ``GATE_STATES``:
103	#   pinned_executable  — every hard term is span-verified + (Phase D) has a
104	#                        capability; ready to enforce.
105	#   degraded_lossless  — LLM ENRICHMENT was thin/failed, but EVERY deterministic
106	#                        explicit constraint survived (nothing vanished).
107	#   blocked_unsupported— a hard term is opaque / has no executable path; preserved
108	#                        + disclosed, never claimed compliant (Phase D completes).
109	GATE_ENFORCEMENT_PINNED = "pinned_executable"
110	GATE_ENFORCEMENT_DEGRADED = "degraded_lossless"

115	# The small policy model — the same arm FS query-gen / the judges use. Overridable
116	# via env so it tracks the lock rather than pinning a slug that could drift.
117	_DEFAULT_POLICY_MODEL = "z-ai/glm-5.2"

119	# I-gate-089 / FX-01 (drb_72 live probe): the policy model (z-ai/glm-5.2) is
120	# REASONING-FIRST — it is in openrouter_client._ALWAYS_REASON_MODELS, so a
121	# generate() call runs its reasoning prelude at effort=high on the SAME
122	# overall max_tokens budget and only floors that budget to PG_GLM5_MIN_MAX_TOKENS
123	# (4096). On a compound prompt (task 72) the reasoning prelude ate the whole
124	# 8192 budget: candidate content reached 14022 then 16546 chars and STILL hit
125	# finish_reason='length' (always_reason_promotion truncation), so the compile
126	# retried and fell to the conservative fallback. Sol's design (§4) says the
127	# ~2000-token cap is inadequate and to use the PROVIDER-RESOLVED MAXIMUM for a
128	# reasoning-first model. Mirror the champion's reasoning-first budget convention
129	# (multi_section_generator / analyst_synthesis): give the compile a real content
130	# ceiling AND bound the reasoning POOL so a fixed content slice always survives.
131	# The client further clamps DOWN to the provider completion cap (B10 resolver),
132	# so this can never over-request. Env-tunable; the fail-soft conservative-contract
133	# fallback stays the backstop when the compile still fails.
134	_CONTRACT_MAX_TOKENS = int(
135	    os.getenv("PG_PLANNING_GATE_MAX_TOKENS", "32768")
136	)
137	_PLAN_MAX_TOKENS = int(
138	    os.getenv("PG_PLANNING_GATE_MAX_TOKENS", "32768")
139	)

141	# reserve the remainder of the budget for content. glm-5.2 branch-1 honors a
142	# caller-passed reasoning.max_tokens (openrouter_client ~line 1958), so this
143	# guarantees content headroom = max_tokens - this pool. Env-tunable (LAW VI).
144	_PLANNING_GATE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS = int(

164	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
165	# System prompts (design §3 / §4 — no-invention discipline is IN the prompt AND
166	# re-checked by the deterministic validators)
167	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
168	
169	_CONTRACT_SYSTEM_PROMPT = (
170	    "You are the POLARIS Research Contract Compiler. Convert the user's complete "
171	    "request into the supplied ResearchContract JSON schema. Your output is a "
172	    "contract for a research system, NOT the answer to the research question.\n\n"
173	    "Rules:\n"
174	    "1. Account for every imperative, question, entity, comparison dimension, "
175	    "scope phrase, source rule, exclusion, format request, and rhetorical "
176	    "instruction in the request.\n"
177	    "2. A term marked origin=\"explicit\" MUST include the exact VERBATIM QUOTE it "
178	    "is based on (copy the phrase from the request; DO NOT compute character "
179	    "offsets — the system re-derives them from your quote). Do not mark "
180	    "implications or world knowledge as explicit.\n"
181	    "3. NEVER invent a restriction. If date, geography, jurisdiction, source "
182	    "type, source language, length, audience, or format is unspecified, "
183	    "represent it as open/null.\n"
184	    "4. Inferred and policy-default terms may ONLY be force=\"preference\" or "
185	    "force=\"open\", NEVER force=\"hard\". Only explicit text or an affirmative "
186	    "user action may back a hard term. List every inferred/default interpretation "
187	    "in assumptions and explain its consequence.\n"
188	    "5. Distinguish source language from output language. Distinguish required "
189	    "topics from required section headings. Distinguish content scope from "

192	    "Preserve dependencies (evidence first, comparison second, recommendation "
193	    "last).\n"
194	    "7. Identify ambiguity rather than resolving it invisibly. Mark whether it "

199	    "section-count quotas unless the user supplied them.\n"
200	    "10. Return JSON only, conforming exactly to the schema. Do not research or "
201	    "answer.\n\n"
202	    "Return a JSON object with keys: contract (the ResearchContract) and "
203	    "clause_coverage (an array dispositioning every operative clause: "
204	    "{span, term_ids, disposition in [represented|non_instructional|ambiguous], "
205	    "explanation}).\n"
206	    "The ResearchContract has keys: schema_version, objective[], scope[], "
207	    "content_terms[], deliverable[], coverage[], sections[], ambiguities[], "

209	    "{term_id, dimension, value, origin, force, confidence, spans[], rationale, "
210	    "enforcement_stages[]}. spans[] may be verbatim QUOTE STRINGS (offsets are "
211	    "derived for you) or {start,end,quote} objects. origin in [explicit, "
212	    "user_answer, inferred, policy_default]; force in [hard, preference, open]."

216	    "You are the POLARIS Research Plan Compiler. Produce ResearchExecutionPlan "
217	    "JSON for the pinned ResearchContract. Do NOT answer the research question "
218	    "and do NOT change the contract.\n\n"
219	    "Rules:\n"

221	    "binding content requirement; scale with complexity rather than targeting a "

242	    "stable semantic section IDs and revisable display titles.\n"

251	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
252	# Gate result
253	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
254	
255	class GateResult:
256	    """The gate's return: an artifact plus mode/state and any diagnostics.
257	
258	    ``needs_input`` is True ONLY in interactive mode with material questions; in
259	    autonomous mode it is ALWAYS False (the load-bearing invariant).
260	    """
261	
262	    def __init__(
263	        self,
264	        *,
265	        artifact: PlanningGateArtifact,
266	        needs_input: bool,
267	        questions: Optional[list[dict[str, Any]]] = None,
268	        contract_errors: Optional[list[ValidationError]] = None,
269	        plan_errors: Optional[list[ValidationError]] = None,
270	        enforcement_state: str = "",
271	    ) -> None:
272	        self.artifact = artifact
273	        self.needs_input = needs_input
274	        self.questions = questions or []
275	        self.contract_errors = contract_errors or []
276	        self.plan_errors = plan_errors or []
277	        # Honest enforcement state (deliverable 6): pinned_executable /
278	        # degraded_lossless / blocked_unsupported. "" when the gate is OFF.
279	        self.enforcement_state = enforcement_state
280	
281	    @property
282	    def contract(self) -> ResearchContract:
283	        return self.artifact.contract
284	
285	    @property
286	    def plan(self) -> ResearchExecutionPlan:
287	        return self.artifact.plan
288	
289	    @property
290	    def state(self) -> str:
291	        return self.artifact.state
292	
293	
294	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
295	# JSON extraction (reuse fence-tolerant discipline from constraint_extractor)
296	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
297	
298	def _extract_json_object(raw: str) -> str:
299	    """First balanced ``{...}`` object substring, fence-tolerant."""
300	    text = (raw or "").strip()
301	    if text.startswith("```"):
302	        text = text.split("\n", 1)[1] if "\n" in text else text
303	        if text.rstrip().endswith("```"):
304	            text = text.rstrip()[:-3]
305	    text = text.strip()
306	    start = text.find("{")
307	    if start == -1:
308	        return text
309	    depth = 0
310	    in_str = False
311	    esc = False
312	    for i in range(start, len(text)):
313	        ch = text[i]
314	        if in_str:
315	            if esc:
316	                esc = False
317	            elif ch == "\\":
318	                esc = True
319	            elif ch == '"':
320	                in_str = False
321	            continue
322	        if ch == '"':
323	            in_str = True
324	        elif ch == "{":
325	            depth += 1
326	        elif ch == "}":
327	            depth -= 1
328	            if depth == 0:
329	                return text[start:i + 1]
330	    return text[start:]
331	
332	
333	def _loads(raw: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
334	    obj = _extract_json_object(raw)
335	    data = json.loads(obj)
336	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
337	        raise ValueError("model output was not a JSON object")
338	    return data
339	
340	
341	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
342	# Candidate → prompt block (seed the compiler with deterministic spans)
343	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
344	
345	def _candidates_block(candidates: list[CandidateConstraint]) -> str:
346	    payload = [c.to_dict() for c in candidates]
347	    return json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=None)
348	
349	
350	def _contract_user_prompt(
351	    prompt: str, candidates: list[CandidateConstraint], mode: str
352	) -> str:
353	    today = _dt.date.today().isoformat()
354	    return (
355	        f"CURRENT UTC DATE (context only, NOT a user constraint): {today}\n"
356	        f"MODE: {mode}\n\n"
357	        f"DETERMINISTIC CANDIDATES (AUTHORITATIVE — already extracted by "
358	        f"deterministic code with verbatim spans). You MUST represent EVERY "
359	        f"span-verified candidate; you may NOT drop, weaken, or re-file one. Your "
360	        f"job is ADDITIVE: classify any UNSEEN phrasing the extractor missed, "
361	        f"decompose the objective into coverage/threads, and enrich — referencing "
362	        f"the candidate values. The deterministic layer owns explicit constraints; "
363	        f"it overrides you on overlap:\n"
364	        f"{_candidates_block(candidates)}\n\n"
365	        f"EXACT USER REQUEST:\n\"\"\"\n{prompt}\n\"\"\"\n\n"
366	        f"Return the JSON object now (contract + clause_coverage)."
367	    )
368	
369	
370	def _plan_user_prompt(contract: ResearchContract) -> str:
371	    return (
372	        "PINNED RESEARCH CONTRACT (do not change it):\n"
373	        f"{json.dumps(contract.to_dict(), ensure_ascii=False)}\n\n"
374	        "Return the ResearchExecutionPlan JSON object now."
375	    )
376	
377	
378	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
379	# Deterministic conservative fallback (never a guessed contract)
380	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
381	
382	def _conservative_contract(
383	    prompt: str, candidates: list[CandidateConstraint]
384	) -> ResearchContract:
385	    """The LOSSLESS fallback core when the LLM output is unusable twice.
386	
387	    When PG_GATE is ON this is the registry-driven lossless core
388	    (:func:`_lossless_fallback_contract`): EVERY deterministic explicit constraint
389	    survives with its canonical dimension and correct stage owner (no category
390	    leakage), and an unmappable kind is kept as a first-class OPAQUE term — so
391	    "degraded" means ENRICHMENT is thin, NEVER that a stated constraint vanished.
392	
393	    When PG_GATE is OFF this is the legacy conservative fallback (byte-identical to
394	    the pre-inversion path): raw prompt as the objective, span-verified candidates
395	    kept, everything else open. It can never invent (only span-backed candidates
396	    become explicit/hard).
397	    """
398	    if gate_enabled():
399	        return _lossless_fallback_contract(prompt, candidates)
400	
401	    objective = [ContractTerm(
402	        term_id="objective.question",
403	        dimension="objective.question",
404	        value=prompt.strip(),
405	        origin=ORIGIN_EXPLICIT if prompt.strip() else ORIGIN_INFERRED,
406	        force=FORCE_OPEN,
407	        spans=[PromptSpan(0, len(prompt), prompt)] if prompt.strip() else [],
408	        rationale="degraded fallback: raw request retained as the objective",
409	    )]
410	
411	    scope: list[ContractTerm] = []
412	    coverage: list[CoverageRequirement] = []
413	    assumptions: list[Assumption] = []
414	    n = 0
415	    for cand in candidates:
416	        span_ok = any(
417	            sp.quote == prompt[sp.start:sp.end] for sp in cand.spans
418	        ) and bool(cand.spans)
419	        # A hard candidate keeps hard force ONLY with a verbatim span; otherwise
420	        # it degrades to preference (the mechanical no-invention guarantee).
421	        force = FORCE_HARD if (cand.force == "hard" and span_ok) else FORCE_PREFER
422	        origin = ORIGIN_EXPLICIT if span_ok else ORIGIN_INFERRED
423	        n += 1
424	        tid = f"scope.cand_{n}"
425	        spans = [PromptSpan(sp.start, sp.end, sp.quote) for sp in cand.spans]
426	        term = ContractTerm(
427	            term_id=tid,
428	            dimension=cand.dimension,
429	            value=cand.value,
430	            origin=origin,
431	            force=force,
432	            spans=spans,
433	            rationale=f"degraded fallback from candidate ({cand.origin})",
434	        )
435	        if cand.dimension.startswith("content"):
436	            coverage.append(CoverageRequirement(
437	                requirement_id=tid, kind="topic", statement=term, required=False,
438	            ))
439	        else:
440	            scope.append(term)
441	        if origin in DISCLOSURE_ORIGINS:
442	            assumptions.append(Assumption(
443	                assumption_id=f"asm_{n}",
444	                statement=f"kept {cand.dimension}={cand.value!r} as an open preference "
445	                          f"(no verbatim span to make it explicit)",
446	                affected_term_ids=[tid],
447	                origin=ORIGIN_INFERRED,
448	                consequence="ranked/routed only; never a hard gate",
449	            ))
450	
451	    return ResearchContract(
452	        objective=objective,
453	        scope=scope,
454	        coverage=coverage,
455	        assumptions=assumptions,
456	        complexity="degraded",
457	        compiler_degraded=True,
458	    )
459	
460	
461	def _lossless_fallback_contract(
462	    prompt: str, candidates: list[CandidateConstraint]
463	) -> ResearchContract:
464	    """The LOSSLESS fallback (PG_GATE ON): the deterministic-authoritative core
465	    run as the WHOLE contract when the LLM failed.
466	
467	    Every span-verified deterministic explicit source/date/exclusion constraint is
468	    authored (via the registry, canonical dimension, correct force), and every
469	    content-coverage candidate becomes a coverage requirement. Deliverable/format/
470	    length/tone candidates land in the DELIVERABLE group — NEVER scope (no category
471	    leakage). An unmappable kind is preserved as a first-class OPAQUE term. Marked
472	    ``compiler_degraded=true``: enrichment is thin, but NO explicit constraint
473	    vanished.
474	    """
475	    objective = [ContractTerm(
476	        term_id="objective.question",
477	        dimension="objective.question",
478	        value=prompt.strip(),
479	        origin=ORIGIN_EXPLICIT if prompt.strip() else ORIGIN_INFERRED,
480	        force=FORCE_OPEN,
481	        spans=[PromptSpan(0, len(prompt), prompt)] if prompt.strip() else [],
482	        rationale="lossless fallback: raw request retained as the objective",
483	    )]
484	
485	    # authoritative source/date/exclusion terms + content-coverage terms.
486	    scope, content = _author_deterministic_terms(candidates, prompt)
487	    coverage: list[CoverageRequirement] = [
488	        CoverageRequirement(
489	            requirement_id=t.term_id, kind="topic", statement=t, required=True,
490	        )
491	        for t in content
492	    ]
493	
494	    # deliverable / rhetoric / doc-type / voice candidates: file by stage owner,
495	    # NEVER in scope. Admit on the CANONICAL dimension against the single reconciled
496	    # admit set (format/length + doc-type + voice dims the compose projection reads),
497	    # so a document_type / kind / tone / audience / pov / hedging preference is
498	    # carried through to ``ComposeRenderProjection.from_contract`` instead of dropped.
499	    # Every admitted term stays FORCE_PREFER (a deliverable shape is never a gate).
500	    deliverable: list[ContractTerm] = []
501	    n = 0
502	    for cand in candidates:
503	        if cand.canonical_dimension() not in _DELIVERABLE_ADMIT_DIMS:
504	            continue
505	        span_ok = _cand_span_ok(cand, prompt)
506	        if not (cand.value or "").strip():
507	            continue
508	        n += 1
509	        deliverable.append(ContractTerm(
510	            term_id=f"deliverable.cand_{n}",
511	            dimension=cand.canonical_dimension(),
512	            value=cand.value,
513	            origin=ORIGIN_EXPLICIT if span_ok else ORIGIN_INFERRED,
514	            force=FORCE_PREFER,   # deliverable shape is a preference, never a gate
515	            spans=_cand_spans(cand, prompt),
516	            subject=cand.subject,
517	            attribute=cand.attribute,
518	            stage_owner=cand.stage_owner,
519	            normalization_status=cand.normalization_status or NORM_EXACT,
520	            rationale="lossless fallback: deliverable shape preserved (render-owned)",
521	        ))
522	
523	    # EXPLICIT, user-named section titles -> SectionRequirement objects (lossless).
524	    # Only candidates whose CANONICAL dimension names a section become sections; a
525	    # required *topic* (content.coverage) is NEVER a heading. exact_title_lock is
526	    # honored ONLY when the title term's origin is in HARD_ELIGIBLE_ORIGINS — we route
527	    # through ``SectionRequirement.from_dict`` so its downgrade rule (no inferred lock)
528	    # is the single source of truth and the constructor cannot bypass it. Sections are
529	    # NEVER manufactured from the kind (kind->role are soft outline preferences, not
530	    # locks). Verbatim title/origin/span/encounter-order preserved.
531	    sections: list[SectionRequirement] = []
532	    s = 0
533	    for cand in candidates:
534	        if cand.canonical_dimension() != "deliverable.section":
535	            continue
536	        title_val = (cand.value or "").strip()
537	        if not title_val:
538	            continue
539	        span_ok = _cand_span_ok(cand, prompt)
540	        s += 1
541	        title_origin = ORIGIN_EXPLICIT if span_ok else ORIGIN_INFERRED
542	        title_term = ContractTerm(
543	            term_id=f"deliverable.section_{s}",
544	            dimension="deliverable.section",
545	            value=title_val,
546	            origin=title_origin,
547	            force=FORCE_HARD if span_ok else FORCE_PREFER,
548	            spans=_cand_spans(cand, prompt),
549	            subject=cand.subject,
550	            attribute=cand.attribute,
551	            stage_owner=cand.stage_owner,
552	            normalization_status=cand.normalization_status or NORM_EXACT,
553	            rationale="lossless fallback: explicit user-named section preserved",
554	        )
555	        # from_dict re-applies the downgrade rule (exact_title_lock only for a
556	        # HARD_ELIGIBLE_ORIGINS title); a claimed lock on an inferred title is dropped.
557	        sections.append(SectionRequirement.from_dict({
558	            "section_id": f"deliverable.section_{s}",
559	            "title": title_term.to_dict(),
560	            "order": cand.detail.get("order") if isinstance(cand.detail, dict) else None,
561	            "required": True,
562	            "exact_title_lock": span_ok,
563	        }))
564	
565	    return ResearchContract(
566	        objective=objective,
567	        scope=scope,
568	        deliverable=deliverable,
569	        coverage=coverage,
570	        sections=sections,
571	        complexity="degraded",
572	        compiler_degraded=True,
573	    )

576	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
577	# DETERMINISTIC-AUTHORITATIVE CORE + MONOTONIC MERGE (Phase B — the inversion)
578	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
579	#
580	# This REPLACES the deleted ``_promote_source_scope`` task-shaped whitelist. The
581	# authority is INVERTED: deterministic code (the S0 candidate adapter + the
582	# candidate→canonical registry) AUTHORS the explicit-constraint contract; the LLM
583	# is additive-only and MERGED monotonically (it may add/enrich, never delete,
584	# downgrade, or re-dimension a deterministic explicit term).
585	#
586	# No per-type branching: every source/date/exclusion/coverage family is driven by
587	# ``candidate_adapter.CANDIDATE_REGISTRY`` (the row supplies canonical dimension +
588	# subject/attribute/operator/stage_owner). An unknown kind stays a first-class
589	# OPAQUE term — preserved, never dropped.
590	#
591	# Gated behind PG_GATE (default OFF): with the flag OFF this whole core is a no-op
592	# and the compiled contract is byte-identical to the pre-inversion path (the LLM
593	# compile alone), preserving the OFF guardrail.
594	
595	# The candidate dimensions whose terms are EXPLICIT authority (a real user rule
596	# with a verbatim span) — as opposed to advisory coverage the LLM may reshape.
597	# A span-verified candidate on any of these is authored as an explicit contract
598	# term the LLM merge can neither drop nor downgrade.
599	_AUTHORITATIVE_DIMENSIONS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
600	    "source.types", "source.quality", "source.language", "source.scope_facet",
601	    "source.jurisdiction", "source.named", "source.named_exclude", "date.recency",
602	    "content.exclusion",
603	})
604	
605	# The DELIVERABLE/compose dimensions the lossless-fallback constructor admits into
606	# ``contract.deliverable`` — SINGLE SOURCE reconciled with what the compose projection
607	# reads (``compose_render_projection._DOCTYPE_DIMS`` + the voice dims) plus format/length.
608	# Imported (not copied) so admit + projection can never silently diverge. These are
609	# FORCE_PREFER preferences filed by stage owner, NEVER scope (no retrieval leak) and NEVER
610	# added to ``_AUTHORITATIVE_DIMENSIONS`` (a deliverable preference there would leak across
611	# STAGE_OWNERS). Matched on ``cand.canonical_dimension()`` so a document_type/kind/tone
612	# variant is admitted after canonicalization (raw ``rhetoric.tone`` canonicalizes to
613	# ``rhetoric.tone`` which is in ``_TONE_DIMS``; ``deliverable.structure`` -> ``_DOCTYPE_DIMS``).
614	_DELIVERABLE_ADMIT_DIMS: frozenset[str] = frozenset(
615	    {"deliverable.format", "deliverable.length"}
616	    | _DOCTYPE_DIMS | _TONE_DIMS | _AUDIENCE_DIMS | _POV_DIMS | _HEDGING_DIMS
617	)

620	def _cand_span_ok(cand: CandidateConstraint, prompt: str) -> bool:
621	    """A candidate carries at least one verbatim-quote-verified span."""
622	    return bool(cand.spans) and any(
623	        0 <= sp.start <= sp.end <= len(prompt) and sp.quote == prompt[sp.start:sp.end]
624	        for sp in cand.spans
625	    )
626	
627	
628	def _cand_spans(cand: CandidateConstraint, prompt: str) -> list[PromptSpan]:
629	    return [
630	        PromptSpan(sp.start, sp.end, sp.quote)
631	        for sp in cand.spans
632	        if 0 <= sp.start <= sp.end <= len(prompt) and sp.quote == prompt[sp.start:sp.end]
633	    ]

636	def _hard_facet_ranges(candidates: list[CandidateConstraint], prompt: str) -> list[tuple[int, int]]:
637	    """Verbatim spans of hard source-KIND scope clauses. A soft source.language
638	    whose span sits INSIDE one of these (e.g. "only cites ... English-language
639	    journal articles") inherits the enclosing "only" hardness — never a
640	    fabricated hard gate (the range must itself be a span-verified hard facet)."""
641	    ranges: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
642	    for c in candidates:
643	        if c.dimension in ("source.scope_facet", "source.types") and c.force == FORCE_HARD:
644	            ranges.extend((sp.start, sp.end) for sp in _cand_spans(c, prompt))
645	    return ranges

648	def _term_from_candidate(
649	    cand: CandidateConstraint, prompt: str, idx: int,
650	    *, hard_ranges: list[tuple[int, int]],
651	) -> Optional[ContractTerm]:
652	    """Author ONE explicit contract term from a span-verified deterministic
653	    candidate, via the registry (generic — no per-type branch). Returns None when
654	    the candidate has no verbatim span (never a fabricated explicit/hard term).
655	
656	    Force: the candidate's own observed force, EXCEPT a soft source.language whose
657	    span is enclosed by a hard source-kind clause, which inherits hard. Origin is
658	    always ``explicit`` (a span-verified user rule). ``normalization_status``
659	    carries through: an opaque candidate becomes an opaque (but preserved) term.
660	    """
661	    spans = _cand_spans(cand, prompt)
662	    if not spans:
663	        return None
664	    row = None
665	    from src.polaris_graph.planning.candidate_adapter import _registry_row  # noqa: PLC0415
666	    row = _registry_row(cand.dimension)
667	    canonical_dim = row.canonical if row else cand.dimension
668	    value = (cand.value or "").strip()
669	    if not value:
670	        return None
671	
672	    force = FORCE_HARD if cand.force == FORCE_HARD else FORCE_PREFER
673	    # language-inside-a-hard-only-clause inheritance (the sole cross-term rule; it
674	    # never invents — it only propagates an already-hard enclosing span).
675	    if canonical_dim == SCOPE_SOURCE_LANGUAGES and force != FORCE_HARD:
676	        for sp in spans:
677	            if any(lo <= sp.start and sp.end <= hi for lo, hi in hard_ranges):
678	                force = FORCE_HARD
679	                break
680	
681	    norm = cand.normalization_status or NORM_EXACT
682	    # An OPAQUE hard term is preserved but cannot be claimed enforceable — it is
683	    # surfaced (the artifact's enforcement state becomes blocked_unsupported).
684	    operator = cand.operator or ""
685	    value_set = [value] if operator in ("IN", "NOT_IN") else []
686	    return ContractTerm(
687	        term_id=candidate_term_id(cand),
688	        dimension=canonical_dim,
689	        value=value,
690	        origin=ORIGIN_EXPLICIT,
691	        force=force,
692	        spans=spans,
693	        subject=cand.subject or (row.subject if row else "source"),
694	        attribute=cand.attribute or (row.attribute if row else ""),
695	        operator=operator,
696	        value_set=value_set,
697	        stage_owner=cand.stage_owner or (row.stage_owner if row else ""),
698	        normalization_status=norm,
699	        enforcement_stages=["retrieval"],
700	        rationale="deterministic-authoritative: span-verified explicit constraint "

700	        rationale="deterministic-authoritative: span-verified explicit constraint "
701	                  "(the LLM merge may enrich but never drop/downgrade it)",
702	    )

704	
705	def _author_deterministic_terms(
706	    candidates: list[CandidateConstraint], prompt: str,
707	) -> tuple[list[ContractTerm], list[ContractTerm]]:
708	    """Author the deterministic explicit contract terms from candidates.
709	
710	    Returns ``(scope_terms, coverage_content_terms)`` — scope/source/date/
711	    exclusion terms vs content-coverage terms — so the caller files each in the
712	    right group with NO category leakage. De-duplicates by canonical term_id
713	    (hard wins over prefer; spans unioned).
714	    """
715	    hard_ranges = _hard_facet_ranges(candidates, prompt)
716	    by_id: dict[str, ContractTerm] = {}
717	    for i, cand in enumerate(candidates):
718	        if cand.dimension not in _AUTHORITATIVE_DIMENSIONS:
719	            continue
720	        term = _term_from_candidate(cand, prompt, i, hard_ranges=hard_ranges)
721	        if term is None:
722	            continue
723	        prev = by_id.get(term.term_id)
724	        if prev is None:
725	            by_id[term.term_id] = term
726	        else:
727	            # merge duplicates: hard wins; union spans.
728	            if term.force == FORCE_HARD:
729	                prev.force = FORCE_HARD
730	            seen = {(s.start, s.end) for s in prev.spans}
731	            prev.spans.extend(s for s in term.spans if (s.start, s.end) not in seen)
732	
733	    scope: list[ContractTerm] = []
734	    content: list[ContractTerm] = []
735	    for term in by_id.values():
736	        if term.dimension.startswith("content"):
737	            content.append(term)
738	        else:
739	            scope.append(term)
740	    return scope, content

742	
743	def _merge_deterministic_authority(
744	    contract: ResearchContract,
745	    candidates: list[CandidateConstraint],
746	    prompt: str,
747	) -> ResearchContract:
748	    """MONOTONIC MERGE: reinstate every deterministic explicit term as authority.
749	
750	    The LLM contract is treated as ADDITIVE. For each deterministic explicit term:
751	      * if the LLM already carries a term on the SAME canonical (dimension,value),
752	        that term is UPGRADED to explicit + the deterministic force (hard wins),
753	        and given the deterministic spans if it lacked them — the LLM enriched it,
754	        the deterministic core owns its force/origin;
755	      * otherwise the deterministic term is APPENDED to the correct group.
756	    The LLM may have ADDED its own terms; those are left untouched. It can never
757	    delete/downgrade/re-dimension a deterministic term — that is the inversion.
758	    Mutates + returns the contract. No-op when PG_GATE is OFF (OFF byte-identical).
759	    """
760	    if not gate_enabled():
761	        return contract
762	
763	    prompt = prompt or ""
764	    det_scope, det_content = _author_deterministic_terms(candidates, prompt)
765	
766	    def _norm_val(value: Any) -> str:
767	        # F7: normalize a value so an LLM alias ("news articles") and the
768	        # deterministic facet ("news_article") dedupe instead of double-surviving:
769	        # casefold, collapse any run of non-alphanumerics to one underscore, drop a
770	        # trailing plural. "News Articles" / "news_article" / "news-articles" all →
771	        # "news_article".
772	        import re as _re
773	        s = _re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "_", str(value or "").strip().casefold()).strip("_")
774	        return _re.sub(r"s$", "", s)
775	
776	    def _key(dim: str, value: Any) -> tuple[str, str]:
777	        return (dim, _norm_val(value))
778	
779	    # index existing scope terms for overlap upgrade.
780	    existing: dict[tuple[str, str], ContractTerm] = {}
781	    for t in contract.scope:
782	        existing[_key(t.dimension, t.value)] = t
783	
784	    for det in det_scope:
785	        key = _key(det.dimension, det.value)
786	        llm_term = existing.get(key)
787	        if llm_term is not None:
788	            # LLM enriched an overlapping term: deterministic owns force/origin/span.
789	            llm_term.origin = ORIGIN_EXPLICIT
790	            if det.force == FORCE_HARD:
791	                llm_term.force = FORCE_HARD
792	            elif llm_term.force != FORCE_HARD:
793	                llm_term.force = det.force
794	            if not llm_term.spans:
795	                llm_term.spans = list(det.spans)
796	            # carry the generic-IR annotations the LLM couldn't be trusted to set.
797	            llm_term.subject = llm_term.subject or det.subject
798	            llm_term.attribute = llm_term.attribute or det.attribute
799	            llm_term.operator = llm_term.operator or det.operator
800	            llm_term.value_set = llm_term.value_set or det.value_set
801	            llm_term.stage_owner = llm_term.stage_owner or det.stage_owner
802	            llm_term.normalization_status = det.normalization_status
803	            llm_term.rationale = (
804	                (llm_term.rationale + " | ") if llm_term.rationale else ""
805	            ) + "deterministic authority upheld (monotonic merge)"
806	        else:
807	            contract.scope.append(det)
808	            existing[key] = det
809	
810	    # content-coverage terms: reinstate as required coverage requirements if the
811	    # LLM dropped them. NOTE: source EXCLUSIONS are NOT here — F1 routes them to
812	    # ``scope.excluded_source_kinds`` (a negative scope predicate handled in the
813	    # scope branch above), never a required CoverageRequirement.
814	    existing_cov_vals = {
815	        _norm_val(cr.statement.value) for cr in contract.coverage
816	    }
817	    for det in det_content:
818	        v = _norm_val(det.value)
819	        if v and v not in existing_cov_vals:
820	            contract.coverage.append(CoverageRequirement(
821	                requirement_id=det.term_id,
822	                kind="topic",
823	                statement=det,
824	                required=True,
825	            ))
826	            existing_cov_vals.add(v)
827	
828	    return contract
829	
830	
831	def _deterministic_candidate_ids(
832	    candidates: list[CandidateConstraint], prompt: str,
833	) -> set[str]:
834	    """The stable term_ids of every span-verified deterministic explicit
835	    candidate — the set ``validate_monotonicity`` requires survived the merge."""
836	    ids: set[str] = set()
837	    for cand in candidates:
838	        if cand.dimension in _AUTHORITATIVE_DIMENSIONS and _cand_span_ok(cand, prompt):
839	            if (cand.value or "").strip():
840	                ids.add(candidate_term_id(cand))
841	    return ids

844	def _enforcement_state(contract: ResearchContract) -> str:
845	    """Derive the honest enforcement state (spec deliverable 6). A hard OPAQUE
846	    term (or a hard term with no executable path) → blocked_unsupported; a
847	    degraded compile → degraded_lossless; else pinned_executable."""
848	    for term in contract.hard_terms():
849	        if term.is_opaque():
850	            return GATE_ENFORCEMENT_BLOCKED
851	    # Phase D seam: a hard term with no registered capability is blocked. The stub
852	    # returns [] today (capability registry lands in Phase D), so this is inert.
853	    if validate_capabilities(contract):
854	        return GATE_ENFORCEMENT_BLOCKED
855	    if contract.compiler_degraded:
856	        return GATE_ENFORCEMENT_DEGRADED
857	    return GATE_ENFORCEMENT_PINNED

860	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
861	# Autonomous disclosure sweep (least-restrictive + every inferred term recorded)
862	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
863	
864	def _autonomous_disclose(contract: ResearchContract) -> ResearchContract:
865	    """Ensure the autonomous invariant: every inferred/policy_default term with a
866	    value has an :class:`Assumption`, and NO hard term survives without an
867	    authoritative origin (defense in depth against a slipped-through hard).
868	
869	    This mutates the contract in place and returns it. It NEVER blocks and NEVER
870	    asks — it is the pure ``contract→contract`` step that lets autonomous mode
871	    proceed with disclosed assumptions instead of questions.
872	    """
873	    disclosed = set()
874	    for a in contract.assumptions:
875	        disclosed.update(a.affected_term_ids)
876	
877	    next_idx = len(contract.assumptions)
878	    for term in contract.all_terms():
879	        # Belt: downgrade any hard term whose origin cannot back a hard force.
880	        if term.is_hard() and term.origin not in HARD_ELIGIBLE_ORIGINS:
881	            term.force = FORCE_PREFER
882	            term.rationale = (
883	                (term.rationale + " | ") if term.rationale else ""
884	            ) + "autonomous: downgraded hard→preference (non-explicit origin)"
885	
886	        if (
887	            term.origin in DISCLOSURE_ORIGINS
888	            and term.value not in (None, "", [], {})
889	            and term.term_id
890	            and term.term_id not in disclosed
891	        ):
892	            next_idx += 1
893	            contract.assumptions.append(Assumption(
894	                assumption_id=f"auto_asm_{next_idx}",
895	                statement=f"assumed {term.dimension}={term.value!r} "
896	                          f"(least-restrictive default; unspecified in the request)",
897	                affected_term_ids=[term.term_id],
898	                origin=term.origin if term.origin in DISCLOSURE_ORIGINS else ORIGIN_INFERRED,
899	                consequence="preference/routing only; never a hard gate",
900	                reversible=True,
901	            ))
902	            disclosed.add(term.term_id)
903	
904	    return contract

908	# Ask-vs-assume (interactive only; ≤3 material questions)
909	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
910	
911	def _material_questions(contract: ResearchContract, limit: int = 3) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
912	    """Generate ≤``limit`` material questions from the contract's ambiguities.
913	
914	    Eligible only if the ambiguity is marked material AND cannot proceed open.
915	    Autonomous mode NEVER calls this. Coupled ambiguities are de-duplicated by
916	    their affected term set.
917	    """
918	    seen_keys: set[tuple[str, ...]] = set()
919	    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
920	    for amb in contract.ambiguities:
921	        if not amb.material or amb.can_proceed_open:
922	            continue
923	        key = tuple(sorted(amb.affected_term_ids))
924	        if key in seen_keys:
925	            continue
926	        seen_keys.add(key)
927	        out.append({
928	            "question_id": f"q_{len(out) + 1}",
929	            "prompt": amb.text,
930	            "choices": list(amb.plausible_interpretations) + ["keep broad / use best judgment"],
931	            "affected_term_ids": list(amb.affected_term_ids),
932	            "why_it_matters": ", ".join(amb.decision_impact) or "materially changes the plan",
933	        })
934	        if len(out) >= limit:
935	            break
936	    return out

943	async def _call(
944	    client: Any, system: str, user: str, max_tokens: int
945	) -> str:
946	    # I-gate-089/FX-01: bound the reasoning-first pool so a fixed content slice
947	    # (max_tokens - pool) always survives — otherwise glm-5.2's effort=high
948	    # reasoning prelude eats the whole budget and content truncates at
949	    # finish_reason='length'. Cap the pool at half the budget so content never
950	    # loses; glm-5.2's branch-1 honors reasoning.max_tokens. Passed positionally-
951	    # safe as a kwarg so the OFF/stub paths (which ignore **kwargs) stay identical.
952	    _reasoning_pool = min(_PLANNING_GATE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS, max(max_tokens // 2, 1))
953	    response = await client.generate(
954	        prompt=user,
955	        system=system,
956	        max_tokens=max_tokens,
957	        temperature=0.0,
958	        reasoning_max_tokens=_reasoning_pool,
959	    )
960	    return getattr(response, "content", None) or ""

963	def _errors_block(errors: list[ValidationError]) -> str:
964	    return json.dumps([e.to_dict() for e in errors], ensure_ascii=False)

967	# Validator error codes that are REPAIRABLE by the autonomous disclosure sweep
968	# (or are advisory), so they must NOT force the conservative fallback / discard a

1224	async def _compile_contract(
1225	    client: Any,
1226	    prompt: str,
1227	    candidates: list[CandidateConstraint],
1228	    *,
1229	    mode: str = "interactive",
1230	) -> tuple[ResearchContract, list[ValidationError], bool]:
1231	    """Contract compile with one bounded correction retry, then conservative
1232	    fallback. Returns ``(contract, remaining_errors, degraded)``.
1233	
1234	    The retry fires on ANY validation error (push the compiler to fix even
1235	    repairable ones). The final keep-vs-fallback decision uses the MODE-AWARE
1236	    fatal set: in autonomous mode an inferred-hard the compiler wouldn't fix is
1237	    downgraded by the disclosure sweep rather than nuking the whole contract to
1238	    the raw-prompt fallback; span-integrity violations stay fatal in both modes.
1239	    """
1240	    user = _contract_user_prompt(prompt, candidates, mode="compile")
1241	    try:
1242	        raw = await _call(client, _CONTRACT_SYSTEM_PROMPT, user, _CONTRACT_MAX_TOKENS)
1243	        data = _loads(raw)
1244	        contract = contract_from_dict(data.get("contract", data))
1245	        # The LLM copies the verbatim quote correctly but drifts on character
1246	        # offsets; re-derive each span from its quote (no-invention: a quote not
1247	        # present in the prompt is left to fail validation). Mirrors the S0
1248	        # candidate adapter's _locate_span discipline.
1249	        reanchor_contract_spans(contract, prompt)
1250	        errors = validate_contract(contract, prompt)
1251	        if not _fatal_errors(errors, mode=mode):
1252	            # No mode-fatal violation. Any remaining errors are repairable (an
1253	            # undisclosed inferred term the autonomous sweep backfills, or an
1254	            # inferred-hard the sweep downgrades), so keep the good contract.
1255	            return contract, errors, False
1256	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
1257	        logger.warning("contract compile failed (attempt 1): %s", exc)
1258	        errors = [ValidationError("compile_exception", str(exc))]
1259	        contract = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
1260	
1261	    # --- one correction retry with machine-readable errors ---
1262	    retry_user = (
1263	        user
1264	        + "\n\nYour previous output was rejected by the deterministic validator "
1265	          "with these errors. Fix EVERY one — especially any hard term that is "
1266	          "not origin=explicit, and any explicit term whose span does not quote "
1267	          "the request exactly. Return corrected JSON only.\n"

1304	        logger.warning("plan compile failed (attempt 1): %s", exc)
1305	        errors = [ValidationError("compile_exception", str(exc))]

1309	        user
1310	        + "\n\nYour previous plan was rejected by the validator with these "
1311	          "errors. Fix EVERY one — give every required coverage requirement a "
1312	          "mandatory query intent via a thread, and bind every hard contract "
1313	          "term in coverage_matrix. Return corrected JSON only.\n"
1314	        + _errors_block(errors)

1318	        data2 = _loads(raw2)

1320	        errors2 = validate_plan(plan2, contract)
1321	        if not errors2:

1343	    if not reqs:

1371	                concepts=[stmt], mandatory=True,

1378	                owning_stages=["retrieval"],

1382	    return ResearchExecutionPlan(
